properties.name: Eurostar
properties.highlight: Drops 75 m below the sea through the 50.5 km Channel Tunnel, 37.9 km of it under the seabed, the longest undersea stretch of any rail tunnel.

properties.name: TGV inOui
properties.highlight: France’s original 1981 LGV forms the first leg; today the TGV holds 320 km/h nearly all the way to the Mediterranean.

properties.name: AVE
properties.highlight: Scheduled at 310 km/h, Europe’s fastest timetable, this line flipped one of the world’s busiest air routes to rail.

properties.name: AVE
properties.highlight: Spain’s first AVE, built for Expo ’92 in standard gauge, the line that reset Spanish railways.

properties.name: ICE Sprinter
properties.highlight: The €10bn VDE 8 project: 26 tunnels and 37 viaducts through the Thuringian Forest halved the Berlin–Munich journey.

properties.name: ICE
properties.highlight: A roller-coaster alignment beside the A3 autobahn with 40 ‰ grades: only the most powerful ICE sets are cleared for it.

properties.name: Frecciarossa
properties.highlight: Italy’s flagship corridor: since 2009 the airlines’ share has collapsed, and the ETR 1000 is engineered for 360 km/h.

properties.name: Eurostar (Thalys)
properties.highlight: The crimson Thalys, folded into Eurostar in 2023, chains French, Belgian and Dutch high-speed lines into one dash.

properties.name: LNER Azuma
properties.highlight: The Flying Scotsman’s route since 1862: and where Mallard set the never-beaten steam record of 203 km/h in 1938.

properties.name: Alfa Pendular
properties.highlight: A tilting Pendolino leaning through the curves of the 19th-century Linha do Norte between Portugal’s two great cities.

properties.name: EIP Pendolino
properties.highlight: Rides Poland’s 1970s Central Main Line (CMK), engineered decades ahead of its time and now being upgraded to 250 km/h.

properties.name: Railjet
properties.highlight: Follows the old Orient Express alignment down the Danube plain: centre to centre faster than any flight.

properties.name: SJ X2000
properties.highlight: Sweden’s tilting flagship since 1990, sprinting the Western Main Line between the country’s two biggest cities.

properties.name: EuroNight
properties.highlight: Scandinavia’s night-train revival: across the Öresund while you sleep, into Germany by breakfast.

properties.name: Santa Claus Express
properties.highlight: Rolls out of Helsinki by night and crosses into Lapland by morning: sleepers and car carriers bound for the Arctic Circle.

properties.name: Venice Simplon-Orient-Express
properties.highlight: Belmond’s restored Art Deco sleepers of the 1920s–30s: black tie encouraged as the Alps slide past the champagne bar.

properties.name: Glacier Express
properties.highlight: “The world’s slowest express”: 291 bridges, 91 tunnels and the 2,033 m Oberalp Pass at an average of 36 km/h.

properties.name: Bernina Express
properties.highlight: A UNESCO World Heritage railway: 7 % grades with no rack, the Brusio spiral, and the highest rail crossing of the Alps at 2,253 m.

properties.name: Bergensbanen
properties.highlight: Northern Europe’s highest main line: over the bare Hardangervidda plateau at 1,237 m, through 182 tunnels.

properties.name: West Highland Line
properties.highlight: Regularly voted the world’s most scenic railway: around Rannoch Moor and over the 21-arch Glenfinnan Viaduct of film fame.

properties.name: Tokaido Shinkansen
properties.highlight: The original bullet train, opened for the 1964 Olympics: the world’s busiest high-speed line, with departures every few minutes past Mount Fuji.

properties.name: Jinghu High-Speed
properties.highlight: The world’s fastest scheduled trains: 350 km/h Fuxing sets covering 1,318 km in under four and a half hours.

properties.name: Trans-Siberian Railway
properties.highlight: The longest railway on Earth: 9,289 km, eight time zones and seven nights between Moscow and the Pacific.

properties.name: Qinghai–Tibet Railway
properties.highlight: The roof-of-the-world railway: 5,072 m over the Tanggula Pass on permafrost, with oxygen piped to every seat.

properties.name: KTX
properties.highlight: Korea’s TGV-derived flagship halved the Seoul–Busan run: today the corridor is one of the most intensively served on Earth.

properties.name: Mumbai Rajdhani
properties.highlight: India’s first Rajdhani: the overnight flagship linking the capital with the financial capital, now in bright Tejas livery.

properties.name: Acela
properties.highlight: America’s fastest train works the dense Northeast Corridor: 240 km/h bursts between five major downtowns.

properties.name: California Zephyr
properties.highlight: Two nights, seven states: through the Rockies’ canyons and over Donner Pass, widely called America’s most beautiful train ride.

properties.name: Rocky Mountaineer
properties.highlight: All-daylight luxury through the Canadian Rockies: glass-domed coaches, an overnight hotel stop and the Kicking Horse Pass.

properties.name: Cusco–Machu Picchu
properties.highlight: Down the Sacred Valley of the Incas beside the Urubamba rapids: the rails are the only road to Machu Picchu.

properties.name: The Blue Train
properties.highlight: A five-star hotel on rails: butlers, marble bathrooms and the Karoo semi-desert rolling past for 1,600 km.

properties.name: Al Boraq
properties.highlight: Africa’s first high-speed railway, named after a mythical steed, holds 320 km/h along the Atlantic between Tangier and Casablanca.

properties.name: The Ghan
properties.highlight: Named for the Afghan cameleers: 2,979 km straight through the Red Centre, completed to Darwin only in 2004.

properties.name: Indian Pacific
properties.highlight: Ocean to ocean across Australia, including the world’s longest railway straight: 478 dead-level kilometres on the Nullarbor.

properties.name: TranzAlpine
properties.highlight: Coast to coast across the Southern Alps: the Waimakariri gorges, Arthur’s Pass and the 8.5 km Otira Tunnel in one morning.

properties.name: TGV inOui
properties.highlight: The LGV Sud Europe Atlantique put Bordeaux’s vineyards two hours from Paris, and emptied the air shuttle.

properties.name: TGV Est
properties.highlight: Where a modified TGV hit 574.8 km/h in 2007: still the world rail speed record for steel wheels.

properties.name: AVE
properties.highlight: Madrid to the Mediterranean paella coast in 100 minutes aboard the duck-nosed “Pato”.

properties.name: AVE
properties.highlight: Splits from the Seville line at Córdoba and dives through the Sierra to the Costa del Sol.

properties.name: Frecciarossa
properties.highlight: Italy’s first true alta velocità line. Vesuvius on the horizon barely an hour after leaving Rome.

properties.name: ICE
properties.highlight: Germany’s busiest city pair rides an alignment opened in 1846, dead straight across the northern plain.

properties.name: Caledonian Sleeper
properties.highlight: The “Deerstalker Express”: fall asleep at Euston, wake up crossing Rannoch Moor with red deer at the window.

properties.name: Flåmsbana
properties.highlight: One of the steepest adhesion railways on Earth: 864 m down to the fjord in 20 km, waterfalls at the window.

properties.name: Rhine Valley Line
properties.highlight: Castle after castle past the Lorelei rock: the left bank of the Rhine is one long UNESCO postcard.

properties.name: Semmeringbahn
properties.highlight: The world’s first true mountain railway (1854) and a UNESCO site: 16 viaducts built before the age of dynamite.

properties.name: Belgrade–Bar Railway
properties.highlight: 254 tunnels, 435 bridges and the vertigo-inducing Mala Rijeka viaduct. Yugoslavia’s engineering swan song.

properties.name: Nightjet
properties.highlight: The founding route of the Nightjet brand: over the Arlberg while you sleep, Alps at both ends.

properties.name: Gotthard Express
properties.highlight: Threads the 57.1 km Gotthard Base Tunnel: the longest and deepest railway tunnel on the planet.

properties.name: Arctic Circle Train
properties.highlight: Crosses the Arctic Circle bound for the aurora: the Iron Ore Line’s fjord finale into Narvik is pure drama.

properties.name: Sapsan
properties.highlight: The “Peregrine Falcon” races the dead-straight 1851 Nikolaev alignment between Russia’s two capitals.

properties.name: EIP Pendolino
properties.highlight: Capital to the Baltic amber coast, Gdańsk’s Hanseatic old town, in under three hours.

properties.name: Railjet
properties.highlight: Follows the Habsburg Empire’s first steam railway of 1839. Prague, Brno and Vienna strung like pearls.

properties.name: EN Ister
properties.highlight: Retraces the original 1883 Orient Express alignment through Transylvania’s castles and the Carpathian passes.

properties.name: Hellenic InterCity
properties.highlight: Past Thermopylae and under Mount Olympus. Greece’s spine line, now run by tilting Italian arrows.

properties.name: InterCity
properties.highlight: Ireland’s premier line since the Famine years: green midlands all the way to the Rebel City.

properties.name: Dovrebanen
properties.highlight: Over the Dovrefjell plateau where wild musk oxen roam: mountain birch, snow fences and midnight sun.

properties.name: Avanti West Coast
properties.highlight: Tilting Pendolinos sway through the Lune Gorge and over Shap summit on Britain’s busiest mixed-traffic artery.

properties.name: GoldenPass Line
properties.highlight: Lake Geneva’s riviera to Lucerne over the Brünig Pass — two trains: the gauge-changing GPX to Interlaken Ost, then zb’s Luzern–Interlaken Express.

properties.name: Jungfraubahn
properties.highlight: Tunnels INSIDE the Eiger to Europe’s highest station at 3,454 m, the “Top of Europe” since 1912.

properties.name: Hokuriku Shinkansen
properties.highlight: Over the Japanese Alps to the samurai gardens of Kanazawa: copper-and-blue E7s built for deep snow country.

properties.name: Hayabusa
properties.highlight: Japan’s fastest trains dive beneath the sea through the 53.9 km Seikan Tunnel to reach Hokkaido.

properties.name: Sanyo Shinkansen
properties.highlight: The Inland Sea corridor past Himeji Castle and Hiroshima, and the home turf of the needle-nosed 500 Series.

properties.name: Kyushu Shinkansen
properties.highlight: Down the volcanic spine of Kyushu toward Sakurajima’s smoking cone: mostly inside tunnels, all speed.

properties.name: Jingguang High-Speed
properties.highlight: The longest high-speed line on Earth: 2,298 km from the Yellow River plain to the Pearl River Delta.

properties.name: Jingha High-Speed
properties.highlight: Cold-proof Fuxing sets built for −40 °C sprint to the ice-festival city of Harbin in Manchuria.

properties.name: Shanghai Maglev
properties.highlight: The fastest commercial train on the planet: 431 km/h with no wheels, floating on magnets.

properties.name: Lanxin High-Speed
properties.highlight: The world’s longest desert high-speed line: wind-walled against Gobi gales on the old Silk Road.

properties.name: THSR
properties.highlight: Shinkansen technology’s first export: the length of Taiwan’s west coast in ninety minutes.

properties.name: Darjeeling Toy Train
properties.highlight: A two-foot-gauge UNESCO marvel looping up to 2,200 m: steam engines older than the tea gardens they pass.

properties.name: Kalka–Shimla Railway
properties.highlight: 102 tunnels and 864 bridges to the Raj’s summer capital: a UNESCO line where the guard still waves flags.

properties.name: Konkan Railway
properties.highlight: Monsoon country: 2,000 bridges and 91 tunnels down the palm-fringed cliffs of India’s west coast.

properties.name: Sri Lanka Main Line
properties.highlight: Through tea country over the Nine Arches Bridge: many call Kandy to Ella the world’s most beautiful ride.

properties.name: Chiang Mai Express
properties.highlight: Thailand’s classic sleeper: dinner past Ayutthaya’s temples, dawn in the teak hills of Lanna.

properties.name: Eastern & Oriental Express
properties.highlight: Cream-and-green carriages gliding past rubber plantations: colonial-era glamour from the Lion City to Siam.

properties.name: Reunification Express
properties.highlight: Two nights along the South China Sea: the Hải Vân Pass clings to cliffs the road never dared.

properties.name: Whoosh
properties.highlight: Southeast Asia’s first high-speed railway: volcano views at 350 km/h across the Parahyangan highlands.

properties.name: Laos–China Railway
properties.highlight: Punched through jungle karst in 167 tunnels, it gave landlocked Laos its first real railway.

properties.name: Afrosiyob
properties.highlight: Central Asia’s only high-speed rail. Spanish Talgos racing to the turquoise domes of the Silk Road.

properties.name: YHT
properties.highlight: From the Bosphorus across the Anatolian steppe. Türkiye’s white Velaros bridge two continents’ doorstep.

properties.name: Haramain High-Speed
properties.highlight: Links Islam’s two holiest cities at 300 km/h through the desert: built to move millions of Hajj pilgrims.

properties.name: Trans-Mongolian
properties.highlight: The Trans-Mongolian’s Gobi crossing: gers, camels, and a bogie change at the Chinese border, where every coach is jacked up for the narrower gauge.

properties.name: Empire Builder
properties.highlight: Two nights tracing the Great Northern’s empire: skirting Glacier National Park along the Montana Rockies.

properties.name: Coast Starlight
properties.highlight: Cascade volcanoes, Bay fog and a hundred miles of Pacific surf from the parlour car’s armchairs.

properties.name: Brightline
properties.highlight: America’s first private intercity railway in a century: bright yellow, 200 km/h through the palms.

properties.name: The Canadian
properties.highlight: Four nights in 1955 stainless-steel dome cars. Ontario lakeland, prairie infinity, then the Rockies at dawn.

properties.name: El Chepe
properties.highlight: Sea level to 2,400 m through Copper Canyon: 37 bridges and 86 tunnels in country deeper than the Grand Canyon.

properties.name: Tren Maya
properties.highlight: A brand-new loop through the Yucatán linking Mayan pyramids. Chichén Itzá to Palenque by rail.

properties.name: Tren a las Nubes
properties.highlight: “Train to the Clouds”: zigzags and spirals up the Andes to the La Polvorilla viaduct at 4,220 m.

properties.name: Serra Verde Express
properties.highlight: Down the Serra do Mar through Brazil’s Atlantic rainforest: an 1885 line of viaducts pinned to green cliffs.

properties.name: Denali Star
properties.highlight: Twelve hours of wilderness with Denali filling the dome windows, flag stops still serve trailside cabins.

properties.name: Andean Explorer
properties.highlight: South America’s first luxury sleeper: over a 4,319 m pass and along Lake Titicaca under Andean stars.

properties.name: Rovos Rail
properties.highlight: “The Pride of Africa”: teak-panelled suites through the bushveld to the smoke that thunders.

properties.name: Nile Valley Sleeper
properties.highlight: Overnight up the Nile past Luxor’s temples: wake to feluccas and the First Cataract at Aswan.

properties.name: Al Atlas
properties.highlight: Across the Haouz plain to the red city below the High Atlas, the classic gateway to the souks.

properties.name: Madaraka Express
properties.highlight: Kenya’s standard-gauge flagship crosses Tsavo on elevated spans built so elephants pass underneath.

properties.name: Addis–Djibouti Railway
properties.highlight: Africa’s first fully electrified cross-border railway runs from the Ethiopian highlands to the Red Sea in a day.

properties.name: Melbourne XPT
properties.highlight: Australia’s InterCity 125 cousin runs the overnight between its two biggest rivals, cricket ashes optional.

properties.name: Spirit of Queensland
properties.highlight: A tilting train with lie-flat “railbeds” running the Coral Sea coast toward the Great Barrier Reef.

properties.name: Northern Explorer
properties.highlight: Volcano country: the Raurimu Spiral climbs to Tongariro’s plateau, Ruapehu steaming beyond the glass.

properties.name: Settle–Carlisle Line
properties.highlight: England’s highest main line crests Ais Gill at 356 m after striding across the 24-arch Ribblehead Viaduct, built by navvies camped on Batty Moss.

properties.name: Night Riviera
properties.highlight: Sleeping cars have run west from Paddington since 1877; today’s train hugs Brunel’s storm-lashed Dawlish sea wall to the far tip of Cornwall.

properties.name: Enterprise
properties.highlight: Jointly run from both capitals since 1947, it rolls over the 1855 Boyne Viaduct at Drogheda and crosses the invisible Irish border without a pause.

properties.name: Nordlandsbanen
properties.highlight: Norway’s longest railway: 729 km of fjordside, forest and bare Saltfjellet plateau, and the country’s only line across the Arctic Circle.

properties.name: Rauma Line
properties.highlight: Corkscrews down Romsdalen past the 1,100 m Trollveggen, Europe’s tallest vertical cliff, via the Stavem spiral tunnel and stone Kylling Bridge.

properties.name: Linha do Douro
properties.highlight: Clings to the Douro’s waterline for some 100 km on ledges blasted out in the 1880s, passing UNESCO-listed port-wine terraces at Régua and Pinhão.

properties.name: Euromed
properties.highlight: Talgo tilting sets swap the Madrid high-speed line for the classic Mediterranean corridor at Camp de Tarragona, running within sight of the beaches.

properties.name: Intercités de Nuit Paris–Nice
properties.highlight: Relaunched in 2021 as heir to the fabled Train Bleu: board at Austerlitz, wake to the red Estérel cliffs and the sea glittering at Saint-Raphaël.

properties.name: Schwarzwaldbahn
properties.highlight: Robert Gerwig’s 1873 blueprint for mountain railways climbs about 650 m through 39 tunnels and two sweeping double loops high above Triberg.

properties.name: Mariazellerbahn
properties.highlight: Opened in 1907 and electrified with its own rare 6.5 kV 25 Hz current in 1911, Austria’s grandest narrow-gauge line crests at 892 m in the Gösing Tunnel.

properties.name: European Sleeper
properties.highlight: Crowdfunded start-up that gave the Low Countries their night train back: via The Hague and Amsterdam to Berlin and the Elbe valley to Prague.

properties.name: Nightjet
properties.highlight: First route to get ÖBB’s new-generation Nightjets in 2023. Mini Cabin pods for solo travellers and 230 km/h sleeper stock.

properties.name: Kyiv–Warsaw Sleeper
properties.highlight: Wartime lifeline to the EU: at the Bug border every carriage is jacked up and its bogies swapped from 1,520 to 1,435 mm gauge while passengers doze.

properties.name: RegioJet Night Train
properties.highlight: Open-access yellow night train since 2015: cheap couchettes out of Prague, over the Jablunkov Pass and under the dark High Tatras to Košice.

properties.name: ICN Zagreb–Split
properties.highlight: Tilting railcars lean through the bare karst of the Lika plateau below Velebit, then swoop down to the palm-fringed Adriatic at Split.

properties.name: Bohinj Railway
properties.highlight: Habsburg Transalpina of 1906: the 6.3 km Bohinj tunnel, the emerald Soča, and Solkan’s 85 m arch, the world’s longest stone rail span.

properties.name: Prahova Valley Railway
properties.highlight: Climbs the Prahova gorge past Peleș Castle at Sinaia to Predeal, Romania’s highest station at 1,032 m, under the Bucegi’s 2,500 m walls.

properties.name: ZSSK InterCity
properties.highlight: Up the Váh valley, then along the 1872 Košice–Bohumín railway: the High Tatras panorama, crowned by 2,655 m Gerlach, rolls past at Poprad.

properties.name: WESTbahn
properties.highlight: Austria’s first open-access challenger, running since 2011: 200 km/h Stadler KISS double-deckers that drove fares down on the Vienna–Salzburg run.

properties.name: Soko
properties.highlight: Serbia’s first 200 km/h railway, built with Chinese and Russian contractors. Novi Sad in 36 minutes since 2022, Subotica since October 2025.

properties.name: Sunrise Express
properties.highlight: Japan’s last regular sleeper; the Izumo half splits at Okayama and takes the Hakubi Line gorges to the sacred San’in coast by breakfast.

properties.name: Tadami Line
properties.highlight: Reopened in 2022 after 11 years of flood repairs; drifts over the mist-wreathed Tadami River bridges through Japan’s deepest snow country.

properties.name: Hong Kong–Beijing High-Speed Sleeper
properties.highlight: Fuxing sleeper EMUs leave Kowloon’s skyline at dusk and cross five provinces overnight, rolling into Beijing West before seven in the morning.

properties.name: Alishan Forest Railway
properties.operator: Alishan Forest Railway & Cultural Heritage Office
properties.highlight: 762 mm trains climb 2,186 m in 71 km via the Dulishan spiral and summit switchbacks; fully reopened in 2024, 15 years after Typhoon Morakot.

properties.name: Nilgiri Mountain Railway
properties.highlight: India’s only rack railway: X-class steam pushes the blue coaches up 1-in-12 grades from palm plains to Ooty’s tea hills; UNESCO-listed in 2005.

properties.name: Vande Bharat Express
properties.highlight: First of India’s home-built semi-high-speed EMUs (2019), skimming the Gangetic plain from the capital to Hinduism’s holiest city in eight hours.

properties.name: Death Railway
properties.highlight: Built by Allied POWs and Asian labourers in 1942–43; trains still cross the River Kwai bridge and creep over the cliff-hugging Wang Pho viaduct.

properties.name: KTM ETS
properties.highlight: Metre gauge yet electric at 140 km/h, among the fastest narrow-gauge trains anywhere, racing past Ipoh’s karst hills to the Penang ferry quay.

properties.name: Tulpar Talgo
properties.highlight: Kazakh-assembled Talgo sets glide low over the steppe, tracking Lake Balkhash’s western shore all night between the old and new capitals.

properties.name: Jerusalem High-Speed Link
properties.highlight: Israel’s first electrified line tunnels and bridges through the Judean Hills to Yitzhak Navon station, 80 m below Jerusalem, in half an hour.

properties.name: City of New Orleans
properties.highlight: Immortalized in Steve Goodman’s 1971 song: overnight down the Mississippi Delta past Memphis, Yazoo City and the Louisiana bayous.

properties.name: Southwest Chief
properties.highlight: Heir to the Santa Fe Super Chief, “Train of the Stars”: crests 2,388 m Raton Pass on the old Santa Fe Trail.

properties.name: Sunset Limited
properties.highlight: America’s oldest train name, running since 1894: crosses the Pecos River High Bridge and the Chihuahuan Desert three times a week.

properties.name: Pacific Surfliner
properties.highlight: Heir to the 1938 San Diegan: surfboards ride free as double-deckers skim the sand at Del Mar and San Clemente.

properties.name: Auto Train
properties.highlight: Cars and motorcycles ride in autoracks behind the sleepers: often the world’s longest passenger train, nonstop Virginia to Florida.

properties.name: Durango & Silverton
properties.highlight: 1882 gold-rush narrow gauge: steam-hauled ever since, on the Highline ledge blasted 120 m above the Animas River gorge.

properties.name: Ocean
properties.highlight: North America’s oldest continuously named train, since 1904: overnight past the Matapédia salmon rivers and Chaleur Bay to the Atlantic.

properties.name: Winnipeg–Churchill
properties.highlight: Two nights across muskeg and permafrost to Churchill, the polar-bear-and-beluga town on Hudson Bay that no road reaches.

properties.name: La Trochita
properties.highlight: Theroux’s Old Patagonian Express: 750 mm gauge, 1922 steam locomotives and wood-stove-heated wooden coaches across the steppe.

properties.name: Trem Vitória a Minas
properties.highlight: Brazil’s busiest long-distance train: daily along the Rio Doce, sharing metre-gauge tracks with 200-plus-wagon iron-ore trains.

properties.name: TAZARA
properties.highlight: Mao-era China’s grandest aid project: the ‘Freedom Railway’ gave landlocked Zambia a copper route that bypassed white-ruled Rhodesia.

properties.name: Dakar TER
properties.highlight: West Africa’s first modern electric railway carried 22.6 million riders in 2024, flying past Dakar’s gridlock at 160 km/h.

properties.name: Lagos–Ibadan Train Service
properties.highlight: Nigeria’s first double-track standard-gauge line; every grand station bears a national figure’s name, from Wole Soyinka to Obafemi Awolowo.

properties.name: Abuja–Kaduna Train Service
properties.highlight: Nigeria’s first standard-gauge railway (2016) links the capital to Kaduna’s Rigasa terminus, far safer than the kidnap-plagued expressway.

properties.name: Gautrain
properties.highlight: South Africa’s 160 km/h Electrostars dive under Sandton’s bank towers, stitching Johannesburg to Pretoria in under an hour.

properties.name: Kuranda Scenic Railway
properties.highlight: Navvies hand-carved 15 tunnels and 37 bridges up Barron Gorge in the 1880s; trains still pause at the falls’ lookout above the rainforest.

properties.name: The Overland
properties.highlight: Running since 1887 as the Intercolonial Express, Australia’s oldest interstate rail journey now crosses the Wimmera wheat belt twice weekly.

properties.name: Coastal Pacific
properties.highlight: Pinched between the Kaikōura ranges and Pacific surf past fur-seal colonies, on a line the 2016 quake lifted bodily out of the sea.

properties.name: Brisbane XPT
properties.highlight: The overnight run still books twin sleepers as it corkscrews through the Border Loop spiral, blasted through rainforest ranges in 1932.

properties.name: The Prospector
properties.highlight: Australia’s fastest diesel railcars shadow the 1903 goldfields water pipeline across the Wheatbelt to Kalgoorlie’s Golden Mile.

properties.name: Joetsu Shinkansen
properties.highlight: Dives under the Tanigawa range into Kawabata’s snow country. Tokyo to metres-deep Echigo-Yuzawa powder and Niigata’s sake coast in under ninety minutes.

properties.name: Akita Shinkansen
properties.highlight: Crimson “Komachi” units ride the Hayabusa at 320 km/h to Morioka, then swap to single-track mountain rails past Lake Tazawa: a bullet train on a branch-line stage.

properties.name: Yamagata Shinkansen
properties.highlight: Japan’s first mini-shinkansen: beyond Fukushima the “Tsubasa” runs re-gauged valley rails below Zao’s slopes into cherry and soba country.

properties.name: Nishi-Kyushu Shinkansen
properties.highlight: Japan’s newest and shortest bullet line. The white-and-red “Kamome” skims Omura Bay to Nagasaki while politics still debates the missing link to Hakata.

properties.name: Kurobe Gorge Railway
properties.highlight: Dam-builders’ narrow-gauge torokko clinging to Japan’s deepest gorge: the full line to Keyakidaira returns in October 2026 after the Noto quake.

properties.name: Hakone Tozan Railway
properties.highlight: Japan’s oldest mountain railway hauls itself up 1-in-12.5 grades through three switchbacks, hydrangeas brush the windows each June.

properties.name: Resort Shirakami
properties.highlight: A rapid that idles on purpose: shamisen players aboard, the Sea of Japan breaking at Senjojiki, and UNESCO’s Shirakami beech forests behind the dunes.

properties.name: Sagano Scenic Railway
properties.highlight: A retired JR alignment above the Hozugawa rapids: twenty-five slow minutes of maples and gorge that the modern main line skips through tunnels.

properties.name: Yufuin no Mori
properties.highlight: Forest-green parlour cars with a buffet of Kyushu treats: over the Mino passes and along the Kusu valley to the boutique onsen town under Mount Yufu.

properties.name: Saphir Odoriko
properties.highlight: All-Green-Car luxury down the Izu coast: nose windows, a fresh noodle bar, and Sagami Bay filling the glass roof beyond Atami’s tunnels.

properties.name: SL Gunma
properties.highlight: Restored D51 498 “Degoichi” and C61 20 haul retro coaches up the Joetsu Line on weekends: steam echoing off the Tone gorge under Tanigawa’s wall.

properties.name: Seven Stars in Kyushu
properties.highlight: Japan’s first luxury cruise train: ten suites and a piano bar on a slow lap of Kyushu’s volcano country; berths routinely go to a lottery.

properties.name: Twilight Express Mizukaze
properties.highlight: Heir to the beloved Twilight Express: emerald art-deco suites and open observation decks drifting along the San’in coast’s empty beaches.

properties.name: Limited Express Hida
properties.highlight: Hybrid HC85s shadow the jade-green Hida River for hours of gorge: riverside onsen at Gero, morning markets at Takayama, then down to Toyama Bay.

properties.name: Limited Express Azusa
properties.highlight: The alpinists’ express. E353s bank through Katsunuma’s vineyards and the Suwa lake basin to Matsumoto’s black castle, gateway to the Japan Alps.

properties.name: Limited Express Sonic
properties.highlight: The blue “wonderland express”: tilting 883s reverse at Kokura and chase the coast past Nakatsu to the steaming hells of Beppu and Oita.

properties.name: Spacia X
properties.highlight: Tobu’s champagne-white flagship to Nikko’s shrine forest: a cockpit lounge, craft beer on draught, and Tokyo Skytree receding from the rear window.

properties.name: Odakyu Romancecar
properties.highlight: Front-row observation seats since 1957, when the SE set a world narrow-gauge speed record. Shinjuku to Hakone’s hot springs without a change.

properties.name: KTX Honam
properties.highlight: Korea’s second high-speed spine: across the Honam rice plain to Gwangju and Mokpo’s seafood quays, where ferries fan out to a thousand islands.

properties.name: SRT
properties.highlight: The upstart from Gangnam. SRT slips out of Suseo through the 50 km Yulhyeon Tunnel and undercuts KTX fares all the way to Busan.

properties.name: KTX Gangneung Line
properties.highlight: Built for the PyeongChang 2018 Games: under the Taebaek passes in a 22 km tunnel, out to Gangneung’s espresso beaches on the East Sea.

properties.name: Baekdudaegan Canyon Train
properties.highlight: White-and-crimson canyon cars crawl the upper Nakdong gorge at 30 km/h: stove-warmed in winter, windows flung open over rapids no road reaches.

properties.name: South-Link Line
properties.highlight: Taiwan’s last main line to be completed: 35 tunnels through the island’s southern spine to Duoliang’s celebrated platform above the Pacific.

properties.name: Pingxi Line
properties.highlight: A 1920s coal branch where railcars squeeze between Shifen’s shop-fronts as sky lanterns lift off: waterfalls and a cat village along the way.

properties.name: Puyuma Express
properties.highlight: Named for the Puyuma people: tilting trains lean through the Yilan curves, past Qingshui’s sheer sea cliffs, into the East Rift Valley’s rice paddies.

properties.name: Mauritania Iron Ore Train
properties.highlight: The world’s longest trains, up to 2.5 km of ore hoppers across the open Sahara, with a single battered passenger coach swaying at the back.

properties.name: Benguela Railway Express
properties.highlight: Atlantic surf to the Congolese border: the Chinese-rebuilt Benguela line’s weekly sleeper climbs 1,344 km through Huambo’s highlands to Luau.

properties.name: Transcamerounais
properties.highlight: Cameroon’s nightly lifeline: couchettes climb from Yaoundé’s hills to the Adamawa Plateau, railhead for the country’s wild north.

properties.name: La Gazelle
properties.highlight: Silenced by war in the Pool, rolling again since 2023: the Congo-Océan line dives through the Mayombe forest to the Atlantic at Pointe-Noire.

properties.name: Algiers–Oran Express
properties.highlight: Algeria’s premier inter-city: bimodal Coradias run the 1871 French-built mainline through the Chélif valley, Algiers to Oran in an afternoon.

properties.name: Algiers–Constantine Express
properties.highlight: East across the high plains of Sétif to Constantine, the “city of bridges” strung over the Rhumel gorge. Algeria’s grandest station approach.

properties.name: Alexandria–Aswan Express
properties.highlight: One seat, the whole of Egypt: from the Mediterranean up Africa’s oldest main line, waking to palms, feluccas and temples along the Nile to Aswan.

properties.name: Al Atlas Night
properties.highlight: Morocco’s night train, out of Tangier at 23:25, couchettes rolling south past Asilah and Casablanca to dawn at the foot of the Koutoubia in Marrakech.

properties.name: Victoria Falls Overnight
properties.highlight: Revived in October 2025: the 1904 line to the Falls leaves Bulawayo at dusk, skirts Hwange’s elephant country and arrives with the spray in sight.

properties.name: Lézard Rouge
properties.highlight: The Bey of Tunis’s 1910 saloon cars, freshly restored: creeping into the Selja gorge’s ochre cliffs, photo stops included, in phosphate country.

properties.name: Tanzania SGR
properties.highlight: East Africa’s first electric railway: 160 km/h from Dar’s Indian Ocean humidity to the capital in under four hours, opened 2024 and already packed.

properties.name: Warri–Itakpe Train
properties.highlight: Begun in 1987 and finished 33 years later: the standard-gauge line linking the Niger Delta’s creeks with the iron hills of Kogi State.

properties.name: Puffing Billy Railway
properties.highlight: Legs out the window over Monbulk Creek trestle: century-old narrow-gauge steam through Dandenongs fern gullies, Australia’s best-loved heritage line.

properties.name: Spirit of the Outback
properties.highlight: Up the coast to Rockhampton, then dead west into Matilda country: sleepers and the Tuckerbox diner, 1,325 km to the stockman’s capital.

properties.name: The Gulflander
properties.highlight: “From nowhere to nowhere” once a week: the 1891 Gulf line’s hollow steel sleepers shrug off every wet-season flood; the old railmotor just keeps going.

properties.name: West Coast Wilderness Railway
properties.highlight: The 1896 copper railway hauls itself up 1-in-16 grades on Abt rack cogs, steaming once more from Queenstown’s bare hills into the King River rainforest.

properties.name: Taieri Gorge Railway
properties.highlight: Out of Dunedin’s bluestone palace of a station and into the Taieri’s schist canyon, where wrought-iron viaducts from 1889 still carry every train.

properties.name: Capital Connection
properties.highlight: The Kāpiti Coast’s own express: one morning run to Wellington under Paekākāriki’s escarpment, home again each evening since 1991.

properties.name: Pichi Richi Railway
properties.highlight: The original Ghan route through Pichi Richi Pass: 1879 stonework, volunteer-run steam and the 1928 “Barwell Bull” railcar to quaint Quorn.

properties.name: Mary Valley Rattler
properties.highlight: A 1920s C17 barks out of Gympie across Deep Creek bridge: the 1915 fruit branch through the Mary Valley, saved twice and steaming on weekends.

properties.name: The Inlander
properties.highlight: Queensland’s grand old night train since 1953. Coral Sea to Cloncurry’s red plains and the smokestacks of Mount Isa, 977 unhurried kilometres.

properties.name: The Westlander
properties.highlight: Over the Toowoomba range by night and out across the Darling Downs: twice a week until the wheat gives way to mulga at Charleville.

properties.name: Byron Bay Solar Train
properties.highlight: The world’s first fully solar-powered train: a 1949 “red rattler” gliding three kilometres of resurrected line to Byron Bay’s beaches.

properties.name: Great Southern
properties.highlight: The Ghan’s summer sibling. Adelaide to Brisbane the long way round, with Grampians sunsets, Canberra and a Coffs Harbour beach stop en route.

properties.name: Shanghai–Kunming HSR
properties.highlight: China’s longest east–west high-speed line: 2,252 km from the Bund to the Yunnan plateau, vaulting Guizhou’s canyons on some of the world’s highest bridges.

properties.name: Southeast Coastal HSR
properties.highlight: D-trains skim 1,450 km of fishing ports, tea hills and sea bridges between the Yangtze and Pearl deltas. China’s window seat on the East China Sea.

properties.name: Chengdu–Chongqing HSR
properties.highlight: Fuxings at 350 km/h stitch the twin megacities of the southwest into 62 minutes: hotpot in Chengdu, riverside neon in Chongqing before it cools.

properties.name: Xi’an–Chengdu HSR
properties.highlight: The first bullet line across the Qinling: 127 km of tunnels under the range that divides north and south China, Terracotta Army to pandas in three hours.

properties.name: Hainan Ring HSR
properties.highlight: The world’s only circular high-speed railway: 653 km looping a tropical island past surf beaches, rocket launch pads and coconut groves.

properties.name: Guangzhou–Hong Kong XRL
properties.highlight: Under the Pearl River Delta in 47 minutes: dim sum in Guangzhou, and the harbour skyline rising over West Kowloon’s station roof by mid-morning.

properties.name: Beijing–Zhangjiakou HSR
properties.highlight: The world’s first autonomous 350 km/h railway, built for the 2022 Winter Games above Zhan Tianyou’s 1909 line: one station lies 102 m beneath the Great Wall.

properties.name: Harbin–Dalian HSR
properties.highlight: The world’s first alpine high-speed line: 921 km from Siberian-cold Harbin to the ice-free port of Dalian on EMUs winterised for −40 °C.

properties.name: Guiyang–Guangzhou HSR
properties.highlight: Eighty-three percent tunnel and bridge: 238 bores through Guizhou’s karst down to the Pearl Delta, four hours where the road once took a day and a night.

properties.name: Lhasa–Nyingchi Railway
properties.highlight: Oxygen-fed Fuxings trace the Yarlung Tsangpo at 3,500 m, crossing the great river 16 times between Lhasa and the peach orchards of Nyingchi.

properties.name: Chengdu–Kunming Railway
properties.highlight: The impossible mountain line of 1970: 427 tunnels and 991 bridges through the Daliangshan, where 5-yuan slow trains still double as the Yi villages’ market bus.

properties.name: Southern Xinjiang Railway
properties.highlight: All-night sleepers skirt the Tian Shan and the Taklamakan’s rim to Kashgar, the last great Silk Road bazaar before the Pamirs.

properties.name: Arktika
properties.highlight: Russia’s polar flagship crosses the Arctic Circle on the 1916 Murman railway: 35 hours north to a port where winter trains arrive in round-the-clock night.

properties.name: Moscow–Kazan Double-Decker
properties.highlight: Two storeys of berths slip out of Kazansky station at 23:08 and pull in below the Tatar kremlin by breakfast. Russia’s double-deck night-train boom, eastbound.

properties.name: Red Arrow
properties.highlight: Departing at 23:55 nightly since 1931 to the strains of ‘Hymn to the Great City’: the crimson Soviet flagship that outlived the state that built it.

properties.name: Circum-Baikal Express
properties.highlight: The Tsar’s ‘golden buckle’: 38 tunnels blasted into cliffs a step above Baikal’s water, once the Trans-Siberian’s costliest link, now a slow shoreline excursion.

properties.name: Baikal–Amur Mainline
properties.highlight: Brezhnev’s ‘Project of the Century’, the Trans-Siberian’s wilder northern twin: 3,800 km of permafrost, seven ranges and towns that exist only for the line.

properties.name: Tbilisi–Batumi Intercity
properties.highlight: Swiss double-deckers roll from the capital through Stalin’s Gori and the Imereti vineyards to palm-lined Batumi. Georgia crossed in five hours.

properties.name: Baku–Tbilisi Night Train
properties.highlight: Back after six years in May 2026: factory-fresh Stadler sleepers with showers and a VIP lounge leave the Caspian at night and wake above the Kura in Tbilisi.

properties.name: Baku–Ganja Express
properties.highlight: Double-deck KISS EMUs since 2021, sprinting the Kura steppe to Azerbaijan’s second city: the quickest thing on rails between the Caspian and the Lesser Caucasus.

properties.name: Yerevan–Tbilisi Night Train
properties.highlight: An every-other-night time capsule down the Debed gorge past Alaverdi’s copper smelters: extended each summer through to the beaches of Batumi.

properties.name: Almaty–Tashkent Talgo
properties.highlight: Central Asia’s new international Talgo, daily since December 2025: dinner leaving Almaty under the Tian Shan, lunch next day in Tashkent.

properties.name: Tashkent–Khiva Night Train
properties.highlight: Khiva had no railway until 2018: now a sleeper crosses the Kyzylkum desert overnight and draws up beside the mud-walled Itchan Kala at breakfast.

properties.name: Issyk-Kul Summer Train
properties.highlight: A summer-weekend crawl up the Boom Gorge to Issyk-Kul, the world’s second-largest alpine lake: panoramic cars bolted on for the mountain walls.

properties.name: Ashgabat–Türkmenabat Night Train
properties.highlight: Down the Tsarist Trans-Caspian mainline of 1886: overnight past Merv’s ruined oasis and the Karakum sands to the Amu Darya’s banks.

properties.name: Gornergrat Bahn
properties.highlight: Europe’s highest open-air railway climbs from Zermatt to 3,089 m: the Matterhorn framed in the window from the first curve to the summit ridge.

properties.name: Pilatus Railway
properties.highlight: The steepest rack railway on Earth: 48 % grades since 1889, with Locher’s horizontal cog wheels gripping a ladder no ordinary train could climb.

properties.name: Rigi Railways
properties.highlight: Europe’s first mountain railway (1871) still ratchets up from Lake Lucerne to the Queen of the Mountains. Mark Twain rode it and wrote home about it.

properties.name: Brockenbahn
properties.highlight: Metre-gauge steam still storms year-round to the 1,141 m Brocken. Goethe’s witches’ mountain, sealed off as a Soviet listening post until 1989.

properties.name: Bayerische Zugspitzbahn
properties.highlight: Germany’s highest railway: cogwheels from Garmisch past the Eibsee, then 4.5 km of tunnel to snowfields at 2,588 m below the country’s highest summit.

properties.name: Centovalli Railway
properties.highlight: Fifty-two km, 83 bridges and 31 tunnels between Piedmont and Ticino: chestnut forests, waterfalls and stone villages a hundred valleys deep.

properties.name: Höllentalbahn
properties.highlight: Germany’s steepest main line climbs Hell’s Valley at 55 ‰: through the Hirschsprung gorge and over the Ravenna viaduct into the high Black Forest.

properties.name: Ffestiniog & Welsh Highland
properties.highlight: The world’s oldest narrow-gauge railway company (1836): double-ended Fairlie steam from Caernarfon castle through Snowdonia to the slate town of Blaenau.

properties.name: The Jacobite
properties.highlight: Steam over the 21 arches of Glenfinnan on the 1901 Mallaig extension: the working train that became the Hogwarts Express, white sands of Morar below.

properties.name: Snowdon Mountain Railway
properties.highlight: Britain’s only public rack-and-pinion railway, Swiss-engineered in 1896: 950 vertical metres from Llanberis to the roof of Wales.

properties.name: Train Jaune
properties.highlight: The canary-yellow metre gauge of the Pyrenees: open-air cars over the Séjourné and Gisclard bridges to France’s highest station at 1,593 m.

properties.name: Le Cévenol
properties.highlight: Down the Allier gorges on viaducts stacked over a river with no road: the Massif Central crossing the TGV era forgot, kept alive by sheer beauty.

properties.name: Ligne des Hirondelles
properties.highlight: 36 tunnels and 18 viaducts swooping like its namesake swallows through the high Jura: stacked stone arches right above the rooftops of Morez.

properties.name: Intercités de Nuit Paris–Briançon
properties.highlight: France’s southern-Alps sleeper: lights out at Austerlitz, blinds up in the Durance valley, arrival under the Vauban walls of France’s highest city.

properties.name: Cinque Terre Express
properties.highlight: Village-hopping between tunnels where Liguria’s cliffs meet the sea. Monterosso, Vernazza, Corniglia, Manarola and Riomaggiore in half an hour.

properties.name: Frecciarossa Milan–Venice
properties.highlight: 300 km/h out of Milan, then Verona, Vicenza and Padua: finishing with the causeway glide across the lagoon to Santa Lucia’s doorstep on the Grand Canal.

properties.name: Nightjet Munich–Rome
properties.highlight: Board after dinner in Bavaria, wake to Tuscan hills: mini-cabins and wireless charging on ÖBB’s newest sleepers, crossing the Tauern by night.

properties.name: Nightjet Zurich–Amsterdam
properties.highlight: Asleep along the Rhine, awake on the IJ: the 2021 revival that re-linked the Alps to the canals, sliding past Cologne’s floodlit cathedral at midnight.

properties.name: Kyiv–Lviv Intercity+
properties.highlight: Launched for Euro 2012 and never stopped: the Hyundai-built Intercity+ keeps capital and Galicia linked across the steppe, through blackout and war.

properties.name: Rantarata Coastal Line
properties.highlight: Finland’s two capitals on the 1903 Rantarata: “coast line” by name, though the track runs mostly inland through granite cuttings and forest. Pendolino Plus sets joined the double-deck ICs in late 2025.

properties.name: Lyntog Copenhagen–Aarhus
properties.highlight: Zealand to Jutland with no ferry: an 8 km tunnel and the 6.6 km West Bridge across the Great Belt made Denmark one rail country in 1997.

properties.name: Sørlandsbanen
properties.highlight: Norway’s south-coast main line, completed in wartime 1944: inland through pine and moor, touching salt water only at Kristiansand and the Jæren flats.

properties.name: Rørosbanen
properties.highlight: The original 1877 inland route to Trondheim: diesel country through Østerdalen’s forests to Røros, the UNESCO copper town still timber-built and soot-dark.

properties.name: Inlandsbanan
properties.highlight: Two summer days up Sweden’s green spine: moose at the window and a trackside stop at the Arctic Circle, where everyone piles out for photographs.

properties.name: Tren de Sóller
properties.highlight: Varnished mahogany carriages from 1912 thread thirteen tunnels through the Tramuntana into Sóller’s orange-grove valley. Mallorca’s rolling time machine.

properties.name: Crescent
properties.highlight: Heir to the 1925 Crescent Limited. Manhattan to the French Quarter past Blue Ridge foothills, midnight Atlanta and a dawn crawl across Lake Pontchartrain.

properties.name: Cardinal
properties.highlight: Amtrak’s slowest way to Chicago and its prettiest: tri-weekly through Virginia horse country and the whitewater canyons of the New River Gorge.

properties.name: Lake Shore Limited
properties.highlight: Heir to the New York Central’s 1897 Water Level Route: up the Hudson at dusk, along Lake Erie by night, into Chicago with morning coffee.

properties.name: Silver Meteor
properties.highlight: The Seaboard streamliner that opened Florida’s diesel age in 1939: 28 hours from Penn Station to palm-lined Miami, still running nightly.

properties.name: Texas Eagle
properties.highlight: A Missouri Pacific name kept alive from the Loop to the Alamo: with through sleepers to Los Angeles, one of the longest runs on the Amtrak map.

properties.name: Amtrak Cascades
properties.highlight: Salish Sea beaches, Puget Sound ferries and Willamette farmland: the Pacific Northwest’s green corridor train, re-equipping with new Airo sets in 2026.

properties.name: Adirondack
properties.highlight: Hudson castles, Lake Champlain narrows and border-country vineyards: widely called the most scenic day train in the eastern United States.

properties.name: Floridian
properties.highlight: Born of a 2024 tunnel-works merger of two classic trains: one seat from Lake Michigan over the Alleghenies to Tampa and Miami, nearly two days aboard.

properties.name: Grand Canyon Railway
properties.highlight: Since 1901 the painless way to the South Rim: vintage Pullmans roll across pine forest and high prairie to a log depot steps from the abyss.

properties.name: Cumbres & Toltec Scenic Railroad
properties.highlight: America’s highest steam railroad, 1880 Rio Grande narrow gauge. K-36 Mikados storm 3,054 m Cumbres Pass and teeter along the Toltec Gorge rim.

properties.name: Mount Washington Cog Railway
properties.highlight: The world’s first mountain-climbing cog railway (1869): still shoving trains up a 37% grade to New England’s stormiest, windiest summit.

properties.name: White Pass & Yukon Route
properties.highlight: Blasted through gold-rush cliffs in 26 months (1898–1900): 900 m of climbing out of Skagway on cantilever bridges, then along Lake Bennett to the Yukon.

properties.name: Pikes Peak Cog Railway
properties.operator: Broadmoor Manitou & Pikes Peak Cog Railway
properties.highlight: North America’s highest railway has climbed to the 4,302 m summit that inspired “America the Beautiful” since 1891; Swiss railcars took over the grade in 2021.

properties.name: Skeena
properties.highlight: Two daylight days from the Rockies to the Pacific. Mount Robson, totem-pole country at Hazelton and the Skeena’s misty canyons, overnighting in Prince George.

properties.name: Polar Bear Express
properties.highlight: No road reaches Moosonee. Ontario Northland’s lifeline train rolls 300 km of spruce and muskeg to tidewater on James Bay, canoes and pickups riding along.

properties.name: Tren Interoceánico
properties.highlight: The 1907 Tehuantepec route reborn in 2023: ocean to ocean across Mexico’s narrowest waist, from Pacific salt winds to the Gulf refineries.

properties.name: El Insurgente
properties.highlight: Mexico’s 160 km/h interurban tunnels out of the Valley of Toluca: since February 2026 running the full 58 km into Observatorio in the capital.

properties.name: Panama Canal Railway
properties.highlight: The world’s first transcontinental railroad (1855): an hour ocean-to-ocean beside the Canal locks, skimming causeways through Gatún Lake jungle.

properties.name: Ferrocarril Central Andino
properties.highlight: A handful of departures a year over the 4,783 m Galera tunnel: zigzags, 58 bridges and oxygen on tap aboard Earth’s second-highest railway.

properties.name: PeruRail Titicaca
properties.highlight: Ten leisurely hours across the altiplano from Cusco to Lake Titicaca: white-linen lunch and an open observation car over the 4,319 m La Raya pass.

properties.name: Tren Patagónico
properties.highlight: Friday nights out of Viedma: sleeper berths, a dining car and an onboard cinema crawl across the Río Negro steppe to the lakes of Bariloche.

properties.name: Tren del Fin del Mundo
properties.highlight: The southernmost railway on Earth: a 500 mm steam line laid by Ushuaia’s prison labourers in 1909, now puffing into Tierra del Fuego National Park.

properties.name: Expreso del Sur
properties.highlight: Tuesdays and Fridays out of Oruro: past Lake Poopó’s salt shores to Uyuni by night, then down the quebradas to the Argentine border at Villazón.

properties.name: Tren Santiago–Chillán
properties.highlight: South America’s fastest scheduled trains: 160 km/h EMUs launched in 2023 sprint down Chile’s Central Valley past vineyards and snow-capped volcanoes.

properties.name: Buscarril Talca–Constitución
properties.highlight: Chile’s last country branch line, a National Monument: a little railbus picks its way up the Maule river gorge to vineyards and hamlets no road reaches.

properties.name: Trem do Corcovado
properties.highlight: Brazil’s first electrified railway (1884): a rack train climbing 700 m through Tijuca rainforest to Christ the Redeemer above Rio.

properties.name: Estrada de Ferro Carajás
properties.highlight: One of the world’s great everyday long hauls: 16 hours through Amazon-edge towns, sharing rails with 300-plus-wagon iron-ore trains bound for Carajás.

properties.name: Tren de la Sabana
properties.highlight: Weekend steam across the Bogotá savanna: brass-era locomotives and papayera bands rolling to Zipaquirá’s underground Salt Cathedral.

properties.name: Howrah Rajdhani
properties.highlight: India’s first Rajdhani (1969): the all-air-conditioned overnight flagship racing the Grand Chord from the capital to Kolkata, dinner and bedrolls at your berth.

properties.name: Bhopal Vande Bharat
properties.highlight: India’s fastest timetabled train: a home-built Vande Bharat touching 160 km/h past Agra and Gwalior, capital to Bhopal in seven and a half hours.

properties.name: Deccan Queen
properties.highlight: Maharashtra’s beloved “Queen” since 1930. India’s first deluxe electric express, still climbing the monsoon-soaked Bhor Ghat with the network’s last dining car.

properties.name: Matheran Hill Railway
properties.highlight: A 1907 two-foot-gauge toy train corkscrewing 21 km up to a car-free hill station: 281 curves, the “One Kiss Tunnel”, and monkeys pacing the carriages.

properties.name: Maharajas’ Express
properties.highlight: IRCTC’s rolling palace: a week-long royal loop from Delhi through Ranthambore’s tigers, champagne breakfast beside the Taj, and evening prayers on the Ganges.

properties.name: Island Express
properties.highlight: Kerala’s workhorse since 1940: overnight from Bengaluru’s plateau down the palm-and-backwater coast to the subcontinent’s southernmost station at Kanniyakumari.

properties.name: Golden Temple Mail
properties.highlight: Born 1928 as the Frontier Mail to Peshawar: the Raj’s most glamorous train still runs daily, Mumbai to the Golden Temple’s city, 1,890 km up the map.

properties.name: Green Line Express
properties.highlight: Pakistan’s premium flagship since 2015: 22 hours up the Indus valley from Karachi’s sea air to Islamabad’s Margalla foothills, meals and bedding included.

properties.name: Khyber Mail
properties.highlight: Rolling since 1920 and named for the pass beyond Peshawar. Pakistan’s grand old mail train, two nights along the Indus, a lounge diner added in 2024.

properties.name: Sonar Bangla Express
properties.highlight: Bangladesh’s premier near-non-stop. Dhaka to the port city of Chattogram in under five hours aboard Indonesian-built carriages with airline-style catering.

properties.name: Cox’s Bazar Express
properties.highlight: Opened 2023 to the world’s longest natural beach: an overnight glide from Dhaka to a seashell-shaped terminus a rickshaw ride from the surf.

properties.name: Janakpur Railway
properties.highlight: Nepal’s only passenger railway, reborn in 2022: a crimson DEMU ferrying pilgrims across the border plain from Jaynagar to Sita’s temple city of Janakpur.

properties.name: Ruhunu Kumari
properties.highlight: Sri Lanka’s first intercity express (1955), so close to the Indian Ocean between Colombo and Galle that spray from the breakers reaches the footboards.

properties.name: Yangon Circular Railway
properties.highlight: A 46 km loop through Yangon life. Monks, hawkers and vegetable baskets share the ex-Japanese railcars, three unhurried hours for pocket change.

properties.name: Bangkok–Vientiane Express
properties.highlight: Since July 2024 the sleeper rolls straight across the Mekong Friendship Bridge, asleep past Ayutthaya, awake in Laos: the first through train from Bangkok into central Vientiane.

properties.name: Hat Yai Special Express
properties.highlight: Thailand’s southern flagship: 945 km down the Kra Isthmus past Hua Hin’s royal station and Surat Thani’s ferry piers, the sleeper gateway to Malaysia.

properties.name: Royal Railway Southern Line
properties.highlight: Weekend trains on Cambodia’s 1960s line to the sea, revived in 2016: through Takeo and Kampot’s pepper country to Sihanoukville’s beaches.

properties.name: Ekspres Rakyat Timuran
properties.highlight: Malaysia’s last sleeper: the overnight “Jungle Railway” through rainforest, limestone gorges and sleepy kampungs from Johor Bahru to the Thai border.

properties.name: Argo Bromo Anggrek
properties.highlight: Pride of Java’s north coast since 1997. Jakarta to Surabaya in under eight hours with two stops, now carrying KAI’s first Suite Class compartments.

properties.name: Argo Wilis
properties.highlight: The great trans-Java day train: curving around the Lebakjero horseshoe under Priangan’s volcanoes, then along the southern rice plains to Surabaya.

properties.name: Bima
properties.highlight: Indonesia’s original air-conditioned night train (1967): the “Biru Malam” still slips out of Jakarta at dusk and glides past Yogyakarta before dawn.

properties.name: Tehran–Mashhad Express
properties.highlight: Iran’s busiest corridor: five-star Fadak sleepers glide 926 km east past desert caravanserai country to Imam Reza’s shrine city of Mashhad.

properties.name: Trans-Iranian Railway
properties.highlight: The UNESCO-listed 1938 masterpiece paid for by taxes on tea and sugar: night trains spiral down the Zagros through a maze of tunnels to the Gulf plain.

properties.name: Baghdad–Basra Night Train
properties.highlight: Iraq back on rails: since 2014 a Chinese-built sleeper DMU hums south past Babylon and the Euphrates towns, Baghdad to Basra by breakfast.

properties.name: Etihad Rail
properties.highlight: The UAE’s first passenger railway, launched June 2026: 200 km/h out of Abu Dhabi and through Hajar mountain tunnels to Fujairah in 105 minutes.

properties.name: Northeast Regional
properties.highlight: Amtrak’s busiest train, over 12 million riders a year, racing the electrified Northeast Corridor at up to 125 mph without the Acela fare.

properties.name: Vermonter
properties.highlight: One of Amtrak’s longest daytime runs; trains reverse at Springfield to swing up the Connecticut River valley into Vermont’s Green Mountains.

properties.name: Downeaster
properties.highlight: Maine’s state-run link passed 10 million riders in December 2024, serving coastal Old Orchard Beach and the outlet town of Freeport.

properties.name: Ethan Allen Express
properties.highlight: In July 2022 it became the first passenger train to reach Burlington in 69 years, extending north from Rutland along Lake Champlain.

properties.name: Maple Leaf
properties.highlight: The only Amtrak–VIA joint train: passengers clear customs at Niagara Falls, where the line crosses the gorge beside the thundering cataract.

properties.name: Pennsylvanian
properties.highlight: Sweeps around Horseshoe Curve near Altoona: the 1854 engineering landmark that finally tamed the Allegheny grade.

properties.name: Piedmont
properties.highlight: North Carolina’s home-grown corridor: the state owns the trains, a fleet of rebuilt 1960s streamliner cars, running four round trips a day.

properties.name: Heartland Flyer
properties.highlight: Oklahoma’s only Amtrak train and the sole daily rail link into Texas, timed at Fort Worth to meet the Chicago–LA Texas Eagle.

properties.name: Carolinian
properties.highlight: At 704 miles the longest state-supported route in the Amtrak system, swapping its electric locomotive for a diesel at Washington.

properties.name: Wolverine
properties.highlight: Hits 110 mph on state-owned track between Kalamazoo and Dearborn: one of the few 100-mph corridors outside the Northeast.

properties.name: Hiawatha
properties.highlight: The Midwest’s busiest corridor: seven round trips a day linking downtown Chicago and Milwaukee in about 90 minutes.

properties.name: San Joaquins
properties.highlight: A Central Valley workhorse whose Thruway buses fan out to Yosemite, LA and the coast; over half of riders connect to a bus.

properties.name: Capitol Corridor
properties.highlight: Amtrak’s fourth-busiest route, along San Pablo Bay from Silicon Valley to the Sierra foothills; one daily train runs the full climb to Auburn.

properties.name: Cass Scenic Railroad
properties.operator: Mountain Rail WV · WV State Parks
properties.highlight: Home to the world’s largest fleet of geared Shay steam locomotives, grinding up 11% grades to Bald Knob, 4,842 ft. West Virginia’s third-highest point.

properties.name: Verde Canyon Railroad
properties.highlight: Two 1953 FP7 diesels, once Alaska Railroad engines, thread a 680-ft curved tunnel and a red-rock canyon that shelters nesting bald eagles.

properties.name: Napa Valley Wine Train
properties.highlight: Guests wine and dine in restored early-1900s Pullman lounge cars behind vintage 1950s Alco diesels, rolling past Napa vineyards at a tasting pace.

properties.name: Great Smoky Mountains Railroad
properties.highlight: The spectacular train-wreck scene in The Fugitive (1993) was staged on this line; the wrecked locomotive still sits trackside near Dillsboro.

properties.name: Georgetown Loop Railroad
properties.highlight: To gain 640 ft between two towns just 2 miles apart, the narrow gauge spirals over itself on the Devil’s Gate High Bridge, an 1884 marvel rebuilt in 1984.

properties.name: Skunk Train
properties.highlight: Nicknamed the “Skunk” in 1925 for its reeking gas railcars, you smelled it before you saw it, still winding out of Fort Bragg through the coast redwoods.

properties.name: Mount Hood Railroad
properties.highlight: Climbs the orchard-filled Hood River valley toward 11,240-ft Mount Hood, reversing up one of just five railroad switchbacks still in regular US use.

properties.name: VIA Rail Corridor
properties.highlight: VIA’s new Siemens Venture trainsets made their Toronto debut on this Ottawa run in October 2024, cruising at up to 160 km/h.

properties.name: VIA Rail Corridor
properties.highlight: Rides the Grand Trunk main line of 1856, which once cut the Toronto–Montreal journey from five days to fourteen hours.

properties.name: VIA Rail Corridor
properties.highlight: The quickest run reaches Québec City’s château-style Gare du Palais in about 2 h 43 min, tracing the south shore of the St. Lawrence.

properties.name: Agawa Canyon Tour Train
properties.highlight: A 10-hour day trip 114 miles north into a wilderness canyon whose blazing autumn colours inspired Canada’s Group of Seven painters.

properties.name: José Cuervo Express
properties.highlight: Pours tequila and stages mariachi as it rolls through UNESCO-listed blue-agave fields to the spirit’s namesake town, Tequila.

properties.name: Tren Francés
properties.highlight: Named for the ex-French TEE coaches France sold Cuba in 2000; Chinese-built cars now run this 854 km island-spanning line, often only every few days.

properties.name: Nariz del Diablo
properties.highlight: To scale the sheer “Devil’s Nose” cliff the line zig-zags up a Z-shaped switchback, edging forward then reversing; reopened in August 2025 after five years shut.

properties.name: Ferrocarril Ezequiel Zamora
properties.highlight: Venezuela’s flagship modern railway bores through long mountain tunnels at 120 km/h to link Caracas with the Tuy-valley town of Cúa in just 31 minutes.

properties.name: Tren a Mar del Plata
properties.highlight: Rails reached the great Atlantic beach resort in 1886; today Chinese-built coaches cross 400 km of pampas from Constitución, packed with summer crowds.

properties.name: Tren de las Sierras
properties.highlight: Revived in 2007, this 1889 line climbs the Punilla valley past Lake San Roque, carrying refurbished ex-Portuguese railcars up into the Sierras de Córdoba.

properties.name: AFE Tacuarembó–Rivera
properties.highlight: Uruguay’s last passenger train. Swedish-built Y1 railcars rattle five days a week through the northern hills to the Brazilian border at Rivera.

properties.name: Express InterCity Premium
properties.highlight: Poland’s fastest train, hitting 200 km/h on the dead-straight Central Rail Line (CMK) engineered for high speed back in the 1970s.

properties.name: Express InterCity
properties.highlight: Historic trunk line on the Paris–Berlin–Warszawa–Moscow corridor; the same rails carry the Berlin-Warszawa-Express onward to Germany.

properties.name: IC Przemyślanin
properties.highlight: Crosses Poland to Przemyśl, the southeastern gateway where standard gauge meets Ukraine’s broad gauge, handover point for Kyiv-bound trains.

properties.name: Kraków–Zakopane
properties.highlight: Climbs from Kraków into the Tatra Mountains on the rebuilt line to Zakopane, Poland’s highest town and winter-sports capital.

properties.name: SC Pendolino
properties.highlight: Czechia’s flagship tilting Pendolino links Prague with Moravia’s industrial capital Ostrava via the cathedral city of Olomouc.

properties.name: RegioJet
properties.highlight: Yellow open-access trains with steward service and free coffee. RegioJet broke the state railway’s grip on the Prague–Brno run.

properties.name: Praha–Cheb Express
properties.highlight: Heads west through Plzeň, birthplace of Pilsner lager, to the spa-country town of Cheb near the German border.

properties.name: Zubačka Rack Railway
properties.highlight: Czechia’s only cog railway, climbing the Jizera Mountains on an Abt rack at a gradient of 58‰, a national record.

properties.name: Tatra Electric Railway (TEŽ)
properties.highlight: Metre-gauge electric cars climb to the High Tatra resort of Štrbské Pleso at 1,320 m, Slovakia’s highest railway station.

properties.name: Balaton InterCity
properties.highlight: Summer expresses to Siófok on the south shore of Lake Balaton, central Europe’s largest lake and Hungary’s beach playground.

properties.name: InterCity Debrecen
properties.highlight: Hourly InterCity across the Great Hungarian Plain to Debrecen, the nation’s second city, on the 160 km/h eastern main line.

properties.name: InterCity Pécs
properties.highlight: InterCity to Pécs, a sun-warmed city of Roman tombs and an Ottoman mosque near the Croatian border in southern Hungary.

properties.name: Children’s Railway (Gyermekvasút)
properties.highlight: A 760 mm line through the Buda Hills staffed almost entirely by children aged 10–14: a Young Pioneer legacy still running today.

properties.name: InterRegio Cluj
properties.highlight: The long haul from Bucharest over the Carpathians via Brașov and medieval Sighișoara to Cluj-Napoca, heart of Transylvania.

properties.name: InterRegio Timișoara
properties.highlight: Follows the Danube past the Iron Gates gorge, then climbs to Timișoara, the Banat city where Romania’s 1989 revolution ignited.

properties.name: Mocănița Vaser Valley
properties.highlight: The world’s last steam-hauled forestry railway still logging, chuffing up the wild Vaser gorge in Maramureș toward the Ukrainian border.

properties.name: InterRegio Sibiu–Brașov
properties.highlight: Runs under the Făgăraș Mountains, Romania’s highest, along the Olt valley between the Saxon citadels of Sibiu and Brașov.

properties.name: BDŽ Fast Train Plovdiv
properties.highlight: Bulgaria’s busiest intercity corridor, being rebuilt for 200 km/h, tying the capital to Plovdiv, one of Europe’s oldest cities.

properties.name: BDŽ Fast Train Varna
properties.highlight: Crosses the Balkan range and the Danubian plain to Varna, Bulgaria’s summer capital on the Black Sea coast.

properties.name: BDŽ Fast Train Burgas
properties.highlight: Runs via Plovdiv and Stara Zagora to Burgas, the port at the heart of Bulgaria’s southern Black Sea beaches.

properties.name: Rhodope Narrow-Gauge
properties.highlight: Bulgaria’s last 760 mm line threads 35 tunnels up to Avramovo, the highest railway station in the Balkans at 1,267 m.

properties.name: Intercity+
properties.highlight: Ukraine’s flagship Intercity+ express, launched for Euro 2012, races across the steppe from Kyiv to Kharkiv via Poltava.

properties.name: Intercity+
properties.highlight: Runs south from Kyiv through the black-earth farmland via the Znamianka junction to Odesa, the grand imperial port on the Black Sea.

properties.name: Intercity+
properties.highlight: Fast Intercity+ from Kyiv to Dnipro, the rocket-building metropolis on the mighty Dnieper in Ukraine’s industrial east.

properties.name: Minsk–Brest Business Class
properties.highlight: Follows the old Moscow–Warsaw corridor to Brest, the fortress city on the Bug where Belarus’s broad gauge meets the EU frontier.

properties.name: Minsk–Gomel Regional Express
properties.highlight: Links the capital with Gomel, Belarus’s second city near the Ukrainian border and the fringe of the Chernobyl zone.

properties.name: Prietenia
properties.highlight: Overnight Friendship sleeper whose coaches are jacked up for a bogie swap at Ungheni. Soviet 1,520 mm broad gauge to European standard.

properties.name: Chișinău–Iași Cross-Border
properties.highlight: A symbolic cross-Prut service reopened in 2021, switching from Moldova’s broad gauge to Romania’s standard gauge at the Ungheni frontier.

properties.name: Zhengzhou–Xi’an HSR
properties.highlight: One of China’s first mountain bullet lines, boring through the loess hills of the Yellow River valley to link Zhengzhou with the ancient capital Xi’an in about two hours.

properties.name: Datong–Xi’an HSR
properties.highlight: Runs the length of Shanxi from the Datong grottoes past walled Pingyao, crossing the Yellow River into Shaanxi to reach Xi’an.

properties.name: Nanning–Guangzhou HSR
properties.highlight: Follows the Xijiang (West River) valley out of Guangxi down to the Pearl River Delta, linking Nanning with Guangzhou.

properties.name: Zhengzhou–Chongqing HSR
properties.highlight: A 1,068 km route crossing the Funiu and Daba ranges, cutting Zhengzhou–Chongqing from about eight hours to under four and a half.

properties.name: Fuzhou–Xiamen HSR
properties.highlight: China’s first 350 km/h cross-sea railway, vaulting three bays on the Taiwan Strait coast to link Fuzhou and Xiamen in 55 minutes.

properties.name: Jinan–Qingdao HSR
properties.highlight: A 350 km/h dash across Shandong from the provincial capital Jinan to the seaside beer city of Qingdao.

properties.name: Hefei–Fuzhou HSR
properties.highlight: Nicknamed China’s most beautiful high-speed line, curving past Mount Huangshan and the Wuyi Mountains through Jiangnan tea country.

properties.name: Shitai HSR
properties.highlight: Burrows under the Taihang range through the 27.8 km Taihang Tunnel, once China’s longest railway tunnel, to join Hebei and Shanxi.

properties.name: Guiyang–Nanning HSR
properties.highlight: 350 km/h through Guizhou–Guangxi karst on a line that is mostly bridge and tunnel, passing the Libo World Heritage limestone scenery.

properties.name: Nanning–Kunming HSR
properties.highlight: Crosses the folded uplands and Nanpan River gorges between Guangxi and Yunnan’s capital, Kunming.

properties.name: Chengdu–Guiyang HSR
properties.highlight: Billed as the first high-speed railway through such deeply folded karst, spanning Jinsha River gorges on some of the world’s highest rail bridges.

properties.name: Yinchuan–Xi’an HSR
properties.highlight: The first bullet line to reach Ningxia, climbing the Loess Plateau and Ziwuling forests from Yellow River Yinchuan to Xi’an.

properties.name: Zhengzhou–Jinan HSR
properties.highlight: A fast diagonal across the North China Plain that crosses the Yellow River to knot together two provincial capitals.

properties.name: Baoji–Lanzhou HSR
properties.highlight: The final link of the east–west corridor to Lanzhou, over 90% bridge and loess tunnel, tracing the old Silk Road past Tianshui.

properties.name: Chengdu–Ya’an Railway
properties.highlight: The first completed stretch of the epic Sichuan–Tibet railway, heading west from Chengdu along the old Tea-Horse Road toward the plateau.

properties.name: Lhasa–Shigatse Railway
properties.highlight: A high-altitude spur off the Qinghai–Tibet line, tunnelling through the mountains to trace the Yarlung Tsangpo canyon to Tibet’s second city.

properties.name: Dali–Lijiang Railway
properties.highlight: Climbs from Dali’s Erhai Lake toward the Jade Dragon Snow Mountain and the old town of Lijiang, nearly all on bridges and through tunnels.

properties.name: Erdenet Railway
properties.highlight: Overnight sleeper north from the capital to the Soviet-built copper city of Erdenet, branching off the Trans-Mongolian near Darkhan.

properties.name: Zamyn-Üüd Express
properties.highlight: The domestic overnight across the Gobi Desert from Ulaanbaatar to the Chinese-border town of Zamyn-Üüd, halting at Choir and Sainshand.

properties.name: Tulpar-Talgo
properties.highlight: A Spanish-built Talgo hauled across the Kazakh steppe between Almaty and the southern city of Shymkent via Taraz.

properties.name: Afrosiyob
properties.highlight: Central Asia’s fastest train, a Talgo 250 racing the Kyzylkum steppe to link Tashkent and Samarkand with Silk Road Bukhara.

properties.name: Uzbekistan Express
properties.highlight: Electrified line into the crowded Fergana Valley through the 19.2 km Kamchik Tunnel, Central Asia’s longest, ending reliance on transit via Tajikistan.

properties.name: Trans-Caspian Express
properties.highlight: The historic Trans-Caspian line along the Karakum Desert’s edge, from Ashgabat to Turkmenistan’s lone Caspian port at Türkmenbaşy.

properties.name: Dushanbe–Kulob Express
properties.highlight: A weekly train made possible only in 2016, when a Chinese-built link finally joined Dushanbe to Tajikistan’s once-isolated southern railway.

properties.name: Zhanghu HSR
properties.highlight: Crosses the Inner Mongolian plateau via Ulanqab; linked to the Beijing–Zhangjiakou line it put Hohhot about two hours from Beijing.

properties.name: ICE
properties.highlight: Races the length of the North German Plain; the dead-straight Hanover–Berlin racetrack, partly laid on old border land, lets ICE 4 sets hold 250 km/h.

properties.name: ICE
properties.highlight: Sprints down the 300 km/h Nuremberg–Ingolstadt high-speed line, one of Germany’s fastest, on gradients so steep that freight trains are banned.

properties.name: ICE
properties.highlight: Runs the Hanover–Würzburg route, Germany’s first Neubaustrecke of 1991, boring through a chain of long tunnels under the hills of Hesse.

properties.name: ICE
properties.highlight: Joins the political and banking capitals via Kassel and the Fulda gap, the Velaro D holding 250 km/h over the wooded ridges of northern Hesse.

properties.name: ICE
properties.highlight: Opened in December 2022, the Wendlingen–Ulm line tunnels under the Swabian Jura and cut the Stuttgart–Ulm leg to just 42 minutes.

properties.name: ICE
properties.highlight: Crosses Germany from end to end, Berlin to the Swiss border, finishing down the Upper Rhine past Freiburg to Basel in about seven hours.

properties.name: ICE International
properties.highlight: Upgraded to new ICE 3neo trains in late 2025, replacing loco-hauled Intercitys on the roughly six-hour cross-border run to the Dutch capital.

properties.name: ICE International
properties.highlight: ICE 3M multi-system sets leave Amsterdam and, past Cologne, dive onto the 300 km/h Cologne–Rhine/Main line with its motorway-style gradients.

properties.name: Moselstrecke
properties.highlight: Follows the wine-terraced Moselle past Cochem, crossing the river on the historic two-tier Alf–Bullay bridge below Germany’s steepest vineyards.

properties.name: Rübelandbahn
properties.highlight: Heritage steam storms a fierce Harz grade once worked by rack rail; the 1926 tank engine 95 027, the Bergkönigin, still climbs to the caves at Rübeland.

properties.name: Molli
properties.highlight: A rare 900 mm-gauge steam line running since 1886, Molli puffs right down the middle of Bad Doberan’s tree-lined avenue on its way to the Baltic shore.

properties.name: Rasender Roland
properties.highlight: Nicknamed the Racing Roland, this 750 mm-gauge steam railway has linked Rügen’s white-sand Baltic resorts of Binz, Sellin and Göhren since 1895.

properties.name: Zittauer Schmalspurbahn
properties.highlight: 750 mm-gauge steam climbs from Zittau to the sandstone crags of the Zittau Mountains, serving the pilgrimage village of Oybin since 1890.

properties.name: Intercity
properties.highlight: The northbound Intercity crosses the young Flevoland polders on the Hanzelijn — the Almere–Lelystad stretch past the Oostvaardersplassen wetlands was voted the Netherlands’ most beautiful train journey by NS passengers in 2016 — then skirts the low Hondsrug ridge to Groningen in a little over two hours.

properties.name: Intercity
properties.highlight: Double-deck VIRM Intercitys run almost the whole length of the country to Maastricht, the old Dutch city on the Meuse near the Belgian border.

properties.name: Intercity Direct
properties.highlight: New ICNG trains take the HSL-Zuid high-speed line, hitting 200 km/h as they link Amsterdam, Rotterdam and Breda without a change.

properties.name: Miljoenenlijn
properties.highlight: Nicknamed the Million Line for its ruinously costly cuttings and viaducts, this steam railway climbs the only real hills in the Netherlands, in South Limburg.

properties.name: Intercity
properties.highlight: One of Belgium’s original 1838 trunk lines, running dead-flat across Flanders through Ghent and Bruges to the North Sea ferry port of Ostend.

properties.name: Intercity
properties.highlight: Terminates beneath Santiago Calatrava’s soaring white vault of steel and glass at Liège-Guillemins, one of Europe’s boldest modern stations.

properties.name: Intercity
properties.highlight: Climbs from Brussels through the forested Ardennes via Namur and Arlon; planned upgrades aim to cut the three-hour run to Luxembourg below two hours by 2030.

properties.name: Intercity
properties.highlight: Traces the route of continental Europe’s first passenger railway of 1835 and ends under the vast domed railway cathedral of Antwerpen-Centraal.

properties.name: Kusttram
properties.highlight: The world’s longest tram line: 67 km of metre-gauge track hugging the entire Belgian coast from De Panne to Knokke, calling at 68 stops.

properties.name: Intercity
properties.highlight: A cross-border Intercity through the wooded Éislek and the Ourthe and Amblève valleys, one of the prettiest rail rides in the Ardennes.

properties.name: RE 11 Mosel
properties.highlight: The RE 11 follows the Moselle from Luxembourg into Germany, boring through the 4.2 km Kaiser-Wilhelm-Tunnel at Cochem to bypass the river’s huge loop.

properties.name: TER
properties.highlight: A short cross-border hop from Luxembourg’s Ville-Haute down to the old French steel town of Longwy, one of the busiest daily commuter runs into the Grand Duchy.

properties.name: Marschbahn
properties.highlight: Reaches the car-free island of Sylt over the 11 km Hindenburgdamm causeway of 1927, a dead-straight embankment laid across the tidal Wadden Sea.

properties.name: TGV inOui
properties.highlight: France’s first high-speed line, opened in 1981: the Paris–Lyon route that launched the whole TGV era, now run in under two hours.

properties.name: TGV inOui
properties.highlight: The 2017 Bretagne–Pays de la Loire high-speed line cut Paris–Rennes to 1 h 25 min, sending TGVs racing across Brittany at 320 km/h.

properties.name: TGV inOui
properties.highlight: TGV Atlantique runs Paris to the Loire estuary city of Nantes in about two hours, topping 300 km/h across the plains of the west.

properties.name: TGV Lyria
properties.highlight: A Lyria Euroduplex glides from Paris into Switzerland at Genève-Cornavin; the 2010 Haut-Bugey line trimmed the run to about three hours.

properties.name: Ouigo
properties.highlight: SNCF’s low-cost double-decker TGV, launched in 2013 with fares from €10, races Paris to Lyon at up to 300 km/h.

properties.name: TGV inOui
properties.highlight: France’s longest domestic TGV run: 300 km/h to Marseille, then the classic Riviera line along the Mediterranean to Nice.

properties.name: TGV inOui
properties.highlight: The dead-straight LGV Nord links Paris to Lille in just 62 minutes at 300 km/h, France’s fastest big-city hop.

properties.name: Intercités
properties.highlight: The Bourbonnais line to the Auvergne volcanoes; new Oxygène trainsets arrive from 2026 to retire its veteran Corail carriages.

properties.name: Intercités
properties.highlight: The cross-country POLT line, Paris-Orléans-Limoges-Toulouse, runs 713 km through the Massif Central on France’s classic north–south artery.

properties.name: Train des Pignes
