properties.name: Railjet Wien–Lienz
properties.highlight: Osttirol’s capital link restored: two railjets a day over the Semmering, through the new Koralm tunnel and up the Drau valley — a child of the Dec 2025 timetable.

properties.name: Jižní expres
properties.highlight: Czechia’s first 200 km/h running: the rebuilt Fourth Corridor past Tábor’s Hussite hilltop to the Budweiser city, a record 85 minutes on the fastest Pendolinos.

properties.name: München–Innsbruck
properties.highlight: The Brenner corridor’s northern approach: out of Munich, across the Inn at Rosenheim, into Tyrol at Kufstein and up the valley beneath the Karwendel wall.

properties.name: Railjet · Deutsches Eck
properties.highlight: Tyrol to Salzburg the fast way — through Bavaria: Austrian expresses cut the German corner nonstop via Rosenheim, Alps on the horizon the whole run.

properties.name: EC Transalpin
properties.highlight: The Transalpin’s Austrian leg, panorama car included: the Gesäuse gates, the Ennstal, Zell am See and the Kitzbühel Alps — Austria crossed the slow, beautiful way.

properties.name: IC Hamburg–Köln
properties.highlight: Germany’s busiest conventional corridor: ports to Ruhr through Bremen, Münster and five more big cities — twice an hour, no high-speed line in sight.

properties.name: IC 1
properties.highlight: Switzerland’s flagship express and spine of the clockface timetable: Zurich to Geneva across the Mittelland, Bern and Lausanne on the way, every half hour.

properties.name: REX Wien–Bratislava
properties.highlight: Europe’s closest capital pair, an hour apart on the 1848 Marchegg line across the Morava floodplain — two countries, one commuter corridor.

properties.name: EuroCity Hungaria corridor
properties.highlight: The Danube-bend main line: EuroCities every two hours sweeping past Štúrovo and the great river’s knee at Visegrád into Budapest.

properties.name: alex München–Praha
properties.highlight: The only direct Munich–Prague train: a famously unhurried crossing of the Bavarian Forest and Bohemia while the corridor awaits its wires.

properties.name: EuroNight Praha–Zürich
properties.highlight: The Czech–Swiss night link revived in 2023: the Elbe sandstone gorge at dusk, the Rhine plain at dawn, Zurich by breakfast.

properties.name: EuroCity Budapest–Zagreb
properties.highlight: The only Hungary–Croatia day train: along Balaton’s southern shore, over the Drava at Gyékényes and down through Zagorje to Zagreb.

properties.name: EC Hornád
properties.highlight: Budapest’s only direct reach into eastern Slovakia: the little EuroCity that grows at Miskolc, bound for Košice’s Gothic cathedral square.

properties.name: IC Kraków–Wrocław
properties.highlight: Poland’s two great tourist cities linked across industrial Silesia on the 1847 Upper Silesian Railway — the mesh line no Warsaw-bound train covers.

properties.name: IC Budapest–Szeged
properties.highlight: Hourly ICs across the Great Plain to Hungary’s paprika capital and its vast Votive Church — the southern spoke of the MÁV intercity star.

properties.name: Wolsztyn Steam
properties.highlight: Europe’s last everyday steam: commuters behind a 1948 Pt47 out of the working Wolsztyn roundhouse — an ordinary ticket, an extraordinary train.

properties.name: IC 3
properties.highlight: The approach every Glacier and Bernina rider actually takes: along Lake Zurich and the Walensee’s cliff wall to Chur, where the narrow gauge begins.

properties.name: IC Poznań–Szczecin
properties.highlight: Continuation of the Warsaw expresses to the Baltic’s big absent city: Szczecin’s Prussian boulevards and the gateway to Świnoujście.

properties.name: TGV Paris–Luxembourg
properties.highlight: The only direct rail link between the two capitals: 320 km/h across Champagne on the LGV Est, then down the Moselle past Metz’s golden cathedral.

properties.name: Nomad Krono+
properties.highlight: Normandy’s trunk from Saint-Lazare: Lisieux, Caen and Bayeux of the Tapestry, out along the Cotentin to the great naval harbour of Cherbourg.

properties.name: Nomad Krono
properties.highlight: The Impressionists’ line down the Seine: Vernon for Giverny, Rouen’s spires, and the ferro-concrete UNESCO city at the river’s mouth.

properties.name: European Sleeper
properties.highlight: The night train that replaced the lost Nightjet: Paris to Berlin via Brussels three times a week since March 2026, the open-access sleeper’s boldest route yet.

properties.name: Intercités
properties.highlight: The Atlantic link that skips Paris: marshes of the Vendée, La Rochelle’s towers and the Charente to Bordeaux — ridership doubled, contracted to 2036.

properties.name: Intercités
properties.highlight: France’s great west–east diagonal, no Paris required: the Loire at Angers and Tours, Bourges’ cathedral, then Roanne and the Lyon hills.

properties.name: Alvia
properties.highlight: Gauge-changing Alvias to the Basque coast: the 2022 Burgos high-speed line, Vitoria’s green ring and the belle-époque bay of La Concha at the end.

properties.name: Alvia
properties.highlight: Off the high-speed line at Palencia and over the 1866 Reinosa pass — a mountain descent to the Cantabrian Sea and Santander’s curved bay.

properties.name: Avant Eje Atlántico
properties.highlight: Galicia’s rebuilt Atlantic spine: 250 km/h between the rías with Santiago de Compostela halfway — the north–south line Madrid’s radial map forgets.

properties.name: TGV Rhin-Rhône
properties.highlight: France’s only province-to-province LGV: Burgundy to Alsace without a glance at Paris, Dijon and Belfort on the way to the cathedral city.

properties.name: TGV Paris–Brest
properties.highlight: To Brittany’s Atlantic tip: the 2017 LGV to Rennes, then the granite north coast by Saint-Brieuc and Morlaix’s viaduct to the naval roadstead of Brest.

properties.name: Intercités de Nuit
properties.highlight: The Pyrenean pilgrim night train: out of Austerlitz every evening on the classic line through Poitiers and Dax, waking beneath the mountains at Lourdes.

properties.name: Intercity Tirreno
properties.highlight: The Tyrrhenian artery the high-speed network bypasses: Maremma’s coast, Pisa, the marble Apuans and the whole Riviera di Levante into Genoa.

properties.name: Milano–Tirano
properties.highlight: Lake Como’s eastern shore from the train window — Varenna, then the vineyard terraces of the Valtellina to Tirano, where the Bernina Express waits.

properties.name: Bari–Matera
properties.highlight: The only railway to the Sassi: a 950 mm oddity climbing the Murgia plateau from Bari to cave-city Matera — every visitor’s first sight of Basilicata.

properties.name: Avant Madrid–Toledo
properties.highlight: Thirty-three minutes off the Seville high-speed line to the city on the Tagus bend — El Greco’s Toledo as a Madrid day trip.

properties.name: Mr Henderson’s Railway
properties.highlight: The British-built 1892 line snaking up the Serranía de Ronda from Gibraltar bay, then on across olive country to the Alhambra’s city.

properties.name: Rodalies R3
properties.highlight: Climbs from Barcelona to 1,494 m at Toses — the roof of the Spanish network — to meet the Yellow Train across the platform at Latour-de-Carol.

properties.name: Intercidades
properties.highlight: Across the Tagus and into the cork-oak Alentejo: ninety minutes from Lisbon to Évora’s Roman temple and bone chapel.

properties.name: Linha do Algarve
properties.highlight: The Algarve’s east–west beach line: Portimão, Silves’ red castle and the lagoon country into Faro — every summer visitor’s slow shuttle.

properties.name: Thrace InterCity
properties.highlight: Greece’s only train across Thrace: 440 km laid in 1896 for the Salonika–Constantinople railway, threading the Nestos gorge between Drama and Xanthi.

properties.name: Pamukkale Ekspresi
properties.highlight: Heir of the Haydarpaşa night express: 418 km across the Aegean interior via Kütahya, Afyon and Dinar, down to Denizli’s white travertine country.

properties.name: Osoblažka
properties.highlight: Czechia’s last public narrow gauge: 760 mm and 102 curves in 20 km — including the network’s sharpest at 75 m radius — dawdling across Silesia at 40 km/h.

properties.name: Żnin District Railway
properties.highlight: A 600 mm minirailway from 1894 chuffing 12 km through Pałuki to the Iron Age lake fort of Biskupin — archaeologists commute by narrow gauge.

properties.name: Nadmorska Kolej Wąskotorowa
properties.highlight: Opened 1896 and still packing 150,000 riders a summer: metre-gauge cars amble from Gryfice to the Baltic cliffs, Poland’s last coastal narrow gauge.

properties.name: Upper Silesian Narrow Gauge
properties.highlight: Running since 1853 — the world’s oldest continuously operating narrow gauge: 785 mm industrial track turned heritage line across the Silesian coalfield.

properties.name: Mocănița Huțulca
properties.highlight: Bucovina’s oldest mocănița, laid in 1888 for a Munich sawmill: the Huțulca puffs 12 km up the Moldovița valley behind a Reșița-built 764 steam engine.

properties.name: Mocănița Sovata
properties.highlight: From the spa resort of Sovata a 1949 Chrzanów steam engine hauls open cars to Câmpu Cetății — a rescued shard of the 82 km Târgu Mureș–Praid line of 1915.

properties.name: Katakolo–Olympia Train
properties.highlight: Greece’s first provincial railway (1882–83) reborn as a cruise shuttle: a metre-gauge railcar links Katakolo’s quay with the sanctuary of Ancient Olympia.

properties.name: Orava Forest Railway
properties.highlight: Built by POWs in 1915–18 to haul timber: the surviving 3.5 km climbs from Tanečník to Sedlo Beskyd on a five-switchback staircase unique in Europe.

properties.name: Posázavský Pacifik
properties.highlight: Prague’s “Pacific”, the tramps’ weekend railway of the 1920s: ledges above the Vltava and Sázava canyons and the 41 m Žampach viaduct on the way to Čerčany.

properties.name: 6 Eylül Ekspresi
properties.highlight: Heir of the Bandırma boat trains that sped İzmir–Istanbul travellers to the Marmara ferries: relaunched in 2023, 341 km via Manisa and Balıkesir.

properties.name: Northern Trans-Iranian
properties.highlight: The Trans-Iranian’s UNESCO-listed northern arm: night sleepers spiral down the Alborz over the Veresk bridge, from Tehran’s desert rim to the Caspian plain.

properties.name: Weißeritztalbahn
properties.highlight: Germany’s oldest working public narrow gauge (1882): 750 mm steam daily through the Rabenauer Grund gorge and past Malter dam, 350 m up into the Ore Mountains to Kipsdorf.

properties.name: Lößnitzgrundbahn
properties.highlight: Daily steam on Dresden’s doorstep since 1884: out of Radebeul’s villa streets into the wooded Lößnitzgrund, past Moritzburg’s moated hunting palace to little Radeburg.

properties.name: Harzquerbahn
properties.highlight: HSB’s metre-gauge trunk across the Harz: 2-10-2 tank engines storm out of Nordhausen up the Zorge valley to Drei Annen Hohne, the junction where the Brocken steam begins.

properties.name: Berner Oberland-Bahn
properties.highlight: Jungfrau gateway since 1890: from Interlaken Ost the BOB splits at Zweilütschinen — one arm to Lauterbrunnen, this one climbing to Grindelwald under the Eiger’s north face.

properties.name: Waldviertelbahn
properties.highlight: NÖVOG’s 760 mm Südast winds from Gmünd through the granite-and-pine Waldviertel to Groß Gerungs, summer steam grinding round horseshoe bends and over stone viaducts.

properties.name: Murtalbahn
properties.highlight: Austria’s second-longest narrow gauge (1894) follows the young Mur 64 km from Unzmarkt to Tamsweg — 70 km/h diesels on weekdays, steam specials all summer.

properties.name: Chemins de fer du Jura
properties.highlight: Metre gauge across the Franches-Montagnes plateau: horse pastures and firs at 1,000 m from watch-town La Chaux-de-Fonds to Saignelégier, then a wooded ravine descent to Glovelier.

properties.name: Brohltalbahn
properties.highlight: The Vulkan-Expreß ambles at Tempo 20 from the Rhine at Brohl 400 m up into the volcanic Eifel, on metre gauge laid in 1901 to carry the region’s tuff and phonolite.

properties.name: Drachenfelsbahn
properties.highlight: Germany’s oldest rack railway (1883) climbs 220 m in eight minutes from Königswinter past Schloss Drachenburg to the Drachenfels ruin above the Rhine — Siegfried’s dragon mountain.

properties.name: Borkumer Kleinbahn
properties.highlight: Timed to the tide: Borkum’s 900 mm railway (1888) meets every ferry at the Reede pier and rolls 7 km along the dike into the North Sea island’s town.

properties.name: Reblaus-Express
properties.highlight: Named after the phylloxera louse: a heritage diesel with a wine-tavern car trundles from Retz’s vine terraces to the Thaya loop at Drosendorf, weekends May to October.

properties.name: Blonay–Chamby Museum Railway
properties.highlight: Switzerland’s first museum railway (running since 1968): Belle Époque steam and electrics cross the Baye de Clarens viaduct high above Lake Geneva on 3 km of 1902 branch line.

properties.name: Preßnitztalbahn
properties.highlight: Torn up in 1986, relaid 1992–2000 by volunteers: Saxon IV K tanks once more steam the upper Preßnitz valley from Steinbach to Jöhstadt on weekends through the year.

properties.name: Steyrtal-Museumsbahn
properties.highlight: Austria’s oldest 760 mm line (1889): ÖGEG steam ambles down the green Steyr valley from Steyr Lokalbahnhof to Grünburg on summer weekends — Bosnian gauge as it began.

properties.name: Wangerooger Inselbahn
properties.highlight: Deutsche Bahn’s only narrow gauge: the tide writes the timetable as diesels haul ferry passengers across the salt marsh from Westanleger pier to car-free Wangerooge village.

properties.name: KTM ETS3
properties.highlight: Electric at last to the peninsula’s tip: since December 2025 the ETS3 EMUs run Kuala Lumpur–Johor Bahru direct, ending a nine-year rail gap to Malaysia’s second city.

properties.name: Mutiara Timur
properties.highlight: Java’s eastern express since 1973, tracing the north coast past Probolinggo before winding through coffee country to the Bali ferry pier at Ketapang.

properties.name: Rajabasa
properties.highlight: South Sumatra’s all-day economy flagship: nine and a half hours and dozens of halts through rubber, coal and pepper country from Palembang to Bandar Lampung.

properties.name: Sribilah Utama
properties.highlight: North Sumatra’s mainline stalwart since 1978, rolling out of Medan across plantation flatlands to the railhead town of Rantau Prapat.

properties.name: Hotan–Ruoqiang Railway
properties.highlight: The 2022 desert line that closed the world’s first rail loop around a sand sea — 825 km along the Taklamakan’s southern rim on viaducts built to let the dunes blow through.

properties.name: Chuanqing Railway
properties.highlight: China’s mountain railway to fairyland: 200 km/h from the Chengdu plain up the Min valley to Huangshengguan, the new gateway station for Jiuzhaigou and Huanglong.

properties.name: Harbin–Mohe Snow Country Train
properties.highlight: The “Snow Country Train” heads 17 hours north to China’s northernmost station at Mohe, where −40 °C winters, frost forests and the odd aurora reward the ride.

properties.name: S-train (Namdo Ocean)
properties.highlight: Since September 2025 the S-train sweeps the whole south coast from Busan to Mokpo, crossing the brand-new Boseong–Imseongri line past tea fields and tidal bays.

properties.name: Isan Watthana
properties.highlight: Thailand’s smartest sleeper: Chinese-built berths glide out of Bangkok at dusk and wake along the Mun River in Ubon Ratchathani, deep in southern Isan.

properties.name: Sabah State Railway
properties.highlight: Borneo’s only railway, opened to Tenom in 1905: a metre-gauge survivor that squeezes through the roaring Padas Gorge on its jungle climb inland.

properties.name: Gajayana
properties.train: KAI New Generation executive & luxury coaches
properties.highlight: KAI’s flagship sleeper since 1999 leaves Jakarta after dark and skirts Java’s southern volcanoes to reach highland Malang in time for breakfast.

properties.name: A-train (Jeongseon Arirang)
properties.highlight: The Arirang-themed A-train leaves the Jungang main line at Jecheon and threads the Jeongseon canyon to Auraji, where two mountain torrents meet.

properties.name: Rail Cruise Haerang
properties.highlight: Korea’s only sleeper cruise: three days around the entire peninsula from Seoul — Suncheon Bay, Busan, Gyeongju, the Jeongdongjin sunrise — with the train as the hotel.

properties.name: G-train (West Gold)
properties.highlight: The West Gold train ambles down the Janghang line’s tidal-flat coast, foot baths steaming on board, with Daecheon beach and the Geum estuary out the window.

properties.name: Dunhuang Sleeper Y667/668
properties.highlight: The branded “Dunhuang” sleeper leaves Lanzhou at 18:00 and follows the Hexi Corridor’s Great Wall forts overnight, arriving near the Mogao caves by dawn.

properties.name: Golmud–Korla Railway
properties.highlight: Opened 2020 across the Altun Shan between Qinghai and Xinjiang: 1,206 km of salt flats, desert and near-total emptiness — one of the loneliest overnight rides on Earth.

properties.name: Petit train d’Artouste
properties.highlight: Built for the dam works of the 1920s: 500 mm open cars creep along a cliff ledge at 1,940 m from the La Sagette gondola to Lac d’Artouste.

properties.name: Train à Vapeur des Cévennes
properties.highlight: Steam between Anduze and Saint-Jean-du-Gard: across the Gardon on four viaducts, past the giant bamboo of Prafrance — 40 open-window minutes in the Cévennes.

properties.name: Oran–Béchar Night Train
properties.highlight: Algeria’s Sahara sleeper, revived in December 2024: couchettes leave Oran at nightfall and reach Béchar at the Saoura’s edge by sunrise.

properties.name: Le Train Rouge
properties.highlight: France’s longest tourist railway: 60 km from Rivesaltes’ vineyards into the Fenouillèdes gorges beneath the Cathar castles of Quéribus and Puilaurens.

properties.name: Intercités de Nuit
properties.highlight: Asleep out of Austerlitz, awake on the Côte Vermeille: the sleeper threads Collioure, Port-Vendres and Banyuls to the last stop before Spain.

properties.name: Ligne de la Côte Bleue
properties.highlight: An everyday TER on a corniche of 23 tunnels and 18 viaducts: calanque after calanque between L’Estaque and Martigues for a few euros.

properties.name: Panoramique des Dômes
properties.highlight: A Strub rack railway spirals up the dormant Puy de Dôme volcano: 5.3 km and 500 vertical metres to the Roman Temple of Mercury at 1,406 m.

properties.name: Le Grand Tour
properties.highlight: Puy du Fou’s rolling theatre: a Belle Époque sleeper loops 4,000 km around France in six days, actors staging a mystery from Reims to Chenonceau.

properties.name: Intercités de Nuit
properties.highlight: The Aveyron sleeper: off the Limoges main line at Brive, then Rocamadour, the Lot valley and Rodez by breakfast — Albi through-cars on weekends.

properties.name: Velay Express
properties.highlight: France’s highest steam railway: a 1906 Mallet hauls metre-gauge trains across the 1,000 m Velay plateau between Raucoules and Saint-Agrève.

properties.name: Chemin de fer de la Vendée
properties.operator: Chemin de Fer de la Vendée
properties.highlight: Belle-Époque dining on the move: restored CIWL Orient Express diners behind an 030 T steam engine, three viaducts between Mortagne and Les Herbiers.

properties.name: Train des Mouettes
properties.highlight: Steam at 30 km/h beside the Seudre oyster beds: an 1891 Schneider — France’s oldest working steam engine — still hauls the gulls’ train to La Tremblade.

properties.name: Train Thur Doller Alsace
properties.highlight: Sunday steam in the Doller valley: a century-old 030 T ambles from Cernay to Sentheim past storks, orchards and the first folds of the Vosges.

properties.name: Dia Tsara
properties.highlight: Madagascar’s east-coast revival of 2023: once a week, twelve hours from Moramanga through rainforest gorges to the Pangalanes shore and Toamasina.

properties.name: Tren Solar de la Quebrada
properties.operator: Tren Solar de la Quebrada S.E.
properties.highlight: Lithium-battery railcars charged by a solar farm glide through the UNESCO-listed Quebrada de Humahuaca to Tilcara — the first solar-powered train in the Americas.

properties.name: Cercanías AM (FEVE)
properties.highlight: Three metre-gauge trains a day still thread all 119 km of the 1898 Santander–Bilbao railway, winding through green Basque and Cantabrian valleys.

properties.name: Ferrocarril de Riotinto
properties.train: Restored mine coaches; steam on winter Sundays
properties.highlight: Restored mining stock creeps along the blood-red Río Tinto through a Mars-like landscape mined for 5,000 years — Victorian steam hauls it on winter Sundays.

properties.name: Maria Fumaça (VFCJ)
properties.highlight: Brazil’s flagship steam railway: ABPF volunteers fire century-old locomotives up the 1875 Mogiana trunk from Campinas to Jaguariúna every weekend.

properties.name: Caminho de Ferro de Moçâmedes
properties.train: CFM weekly express, restaurant & multimedia cars
properties.highlight: Angola’s third great trunk climbs from the desert port of Namibe over the Serra da Chela to Menongue — 756 rebuilt km, with a weekly express since December 2023.

properties.name: Tren Macho
properties.highlight: Peru’s beloved Tren Macho is back: free trains roll the first 57 km down the Mantaro valley to Cuenca while the century-old Huancavelica line is modernized.

properties.name: Bonde de Santa Teresa
properties.highlight: Latin America’s oldest electric tramway (1896) rattles across the Carioca Aqueduct’s arches and up Santa Teresa’s cobbled lanes high above downtown Rio.

properties.name: Tren de Felipe II
properties.highlight: An ALCO diesel and 1940s coaches staffed by costumed actors climb from Príncipe Pío into the Guadarrama foothills to Philip II’s colossal monastery-palace.

properties.name: Expresso Turístico
properties.highlight: A 1952 ALCO diesel and 1960s Budd cars leave Luz station on weekends for Paranapiacaba, the fog-wrapped English railway village at the rim of the Serra do Mar.

properties.name: Cercanías AM (FEVE)
properties.highlight: Europe’s longest narrow-gauge ride: two trains a day take seven hours to weave around Galicia’s rias and along the Asturian cliffs from Ferrol to Oviedo.

properties.name: Treno Natura
properties.highlight: Steam excursions across the UNESCO Val d’Orcia: 51 km of cypress avenues, clay badlands and Brunello vineyards on an 1865 branch kept alive purely for the Treno Natura.

properties.name: Porrettana Express
properties.highlight: The 1864 Porrettana was Italy’s first railway across the Apennines: E.626-hauled Centoporte coaches now relive its 26 ‰ climb from Pistoia to the spa town of Porretta.

properties.name: Trenino Verde
properties.highlight: The Trenino Verde’s second living branch: 46 km of 950 mm track twisting off Macomer’s basalt plateau down to the palm-lined seafront at Bosa Marina.

properties.name: Irpinia Express
properties.highlight: Closed in 2010, revived for excursions: the 1895 Ofanto-valley line threads 119 km of viaducts, vineyards and hill villages from Avellino to lonely Rocchetta junction.

properties.name: Treno di Dante
properties.operator: Il Treno di Dante · Fondazione FS
properties.highlight: Vintage Centoporte coaches link Dante’s Florence with his tomb in Ravenna over the Apennine Faentina line — joined by new direct Sunday trains since July 2026.

properties.name: Ferrovia Trento–Malè–Mezzana
properties.highlight: A metre-gauge electric line out of Trento: through the Rocchetta gorge into apple-carpeted Val di Non and Val di Sole, ending at Mezzana beneath the Brenta Dolomites.

properties.name: Chemin de Fer à Vapeur des Trois Vallées
properties.highlight: Belgium’s premier steam railway: CFV3V engines bark up the wooded Viroin valley on an 1854 Entre-Sambre-et-Meuse line to the great museum shed at Treignes.

properties.name: Lennakatten
properties.highlight: Steam from the edge of a university city: the 891 mm Lennakatten rolls 33 km through Uppland forests and lakeside summer halts to the engine sheds at Faringe.

properties.name: Krøderbanen
properties.highlight: Norway’s longest museum railway: 26 km of the 1872 Krøder line preserved whole — stations, water cranes and NSB steam between Vikersund and the lake at Krøderen.

properties.name: Gamle Vossebanen
properties.highlight: The 1883 Voss line’s surviving loop outside Bergen: NSB 18c steam between Garnes and Midttun, bypassed by new tunnels in 1964 and kept alive by volunteers.

properties.name: Thamshavnbanen
properties.highlight: Built in 1908 for Orkla’s copper ore: the oldest alternating-current railway still in service, running its original 6.6 kV motor coaches along the green Orkla valley.

properties.name: Setesdalsbanen
properties.highlight: Norway’s oldest museum railway, volunteer-run since 1964: 1890s narrow-gauge steam puffing up the Otra river from Grovane to the little terminus at Røyknes.

properties.name: Veluwsche Stoomtrein
properties.highlight: The Netherlands’ biggest steam operation: wartime German Kriegsloks and a forty-strong fleet work the 1887 Veluwe country line between Apeldoorn and Dieren.

properties.name: Mariager–Handest Veteranjernbane
properties.highlight: Denmark’s leading veteran railway: 1909 Henschel steam from the fjord port of Mariager over rolling moraine hills to Handest, run in 1950s private-railway style.

properties.name: TOHOKU EMOTION
properties.highlight: A restaurant on rails — open kitchen car, chef’s courses at every seat — rolling the Sanriku surf from Hachinohe to Kuji along the Tanesashi coast.

properties.name: Tama Densha
properties.highlight: Eiji Mitooka’s white train painted with 101 cartoon Tamas trundles to Kishi, where calico stationmaster Tama famously saved the line — a cat shrine now guards the platform.

properties.name: SL Banetsu Monogatari
properties.highlight: C57 180 — “the Lady” — hauls seven retro coaches up the Agano river gorge from Niitsu to castle-town Aizu-Wakamatsu, one of Japan’s last full-size mainline steam runs.

properties.name: Setsugekka
properties.highlight: Crimson-and-silver resort train with Japan’s largest train windows, serving regional courses on a slow sweep from Mt Myoko’s snow country to the Sea of Japan at Itoigawa.

properties.name: Iyonada Monogatari
properties.highlight: A retro maroon-and-gold diner idling along the Iyonada shore — brunch above the waves and a pause at Shimonada, Japan’s most photographed seaside platform.

properties.name: Kairi
properties.highlight: Kairi means “sea and village”: a hybrid diner serving Niigata and Shonai delicacies while threading the Sasagawa-nagare, 11 km of wave-gnawed rocks on the Uetsu coast.

properties.name: GRAN Tenku
properties.highlight: Nankai’s deep-red 2026 flagship runs through from Namba’s neon to Gokurakubashi, grinding up 50‰ cedar slopes to the cable car for sacred Koyasan — lounge and bar aboard.

properties.name: Umisachi-Yamasachi
properties.highlight: Obi-cedar railcars named for the sea-luck, mountain-luck myth skim the Nichinan palm coast past Aoshima’s Devil’s Washboard reefs to the fishing port of Nango.

properties.name: A-Train (Amakusa)
properties.highlight: A jazz-soundtracked bar on rails — dark wood, stained glass, “A-Highball” in hand — swinging along the Ariake tidal flats from Kumamoto to the Amakusa ferry at Misumi.

properties.name: HIGH RAIL 1375
properties.highlight: Grinds to 1,375 m at JR’s highest rail point below Yatsugatake — observatory seats by day, a stargazing dome and astronomer-guided night runs under the Milky Way.

properties.name: etSETOra
properties.highlight: etSETOra drifts the Kure Line’s island-studded Seto shore with Hiroshima patissiers’ sweets courses and a sake bar — extended in 2025 to run clear through to Fukuyama.

properties.name: SL Paleo Express
properties.highlight: The closest steam to Tokyo: wartime C58 363 huffs four retro coaches from Kumagaya into the Chichibu hills past Nagatoro’s rock terraces — running since 1988.

properties.name: Koshino Shu*Kura
properties.highlight: A sake brewery on wheels — Niigata tasting bar, live jazz, a long pause on the sea cliff at Aoumigawa — winding via Nagaoka into snow-country Tokamachi.

properties.name: Belles montagnes et mer
properties.highlight: “Beautiful mountains and sea”: an emerald art-gallery railcar where a sushi chef works the counter as Amaharashi bay frames 3,000-metre Tateyama across the water.

properties.name: Blue Symphony
properties.highlight: Navy-and-gold Blue Symphony glides from Osaka Abenobashi to holy Yoshino — UNESCO cherry mountain — with a lounge bar, cake sets and armchairs in a reborn commuter train.

properties.name: Tosa Toki no Yoake no Monogatari
properties.highlight: Ryoma-themed “dawn of a new era” train pairs Tosa seafood with Pacific panoramas above Susaki and Tosa-Kure bays, locals waving it through tea fields to Kubokawa.

properties.name: Hanayome Noren
properties.highlight: A rolling Kaga bridal curtain: vermilion-and-black cars finished in Wajima-lacquer motifs and gold leaf, serving Ishikawa sweets en route to Wakura Onsen’s seaside baths.

properties.name: Rokumon
properties.highlight: Rokumon wears the Sanada clan’s six-coin crest in deep crimson, serving kaiseki under Mt Asama on the old Shinetsu main line from Karuizawa down to Zenko-ji’s Nagano.

properties.name: Ametsuchi
properties.highlight: Lapis-blue Ametsuchi — “heaven and earth” from the Kojiki’s opening line — roams the San’in coast’s breakers past Mt Daisen and Lake Shinji from Tottori to Izumo.

properties.name: Ruskeala Express
properties.highlight: Russia’s only daily steam service: blue retro cars behind a vintage locomotive from Sortavala to Ruskeala’s flooded marble canyon, staged down to conductors in 1900s dress.

properties.name: Night Express
properties.highlight: Ukraine’s overnight hop across the Carpathians: asleep through Lviv, awake for the climb past Skole into the 2018 twin-bore Beskyd tunnel, then down the valley to Uzhhorod.

properties.name: Hutsulshchyna
properties.highlight: Ukraine’s flagship sleeper into Hutsul country: dusk in Kyiv, dawn above the Prut gorge at Yaremche, then Vorokhta’s stone viaduct and the Yablunytsia pass down to Rakhiv.

properties.name: Vorkuta
properties.highlight: Two nights up the Stalin-era Pechora mainline to Vorkuta, a coal city beyond the Arctic Circle that no all-year road reaches — train № 41 is its lifeline to Moscow.

properties.name: Premium
properties.highlight: The southern flagship since 1964 — first Ritsa, then Sochi, Premium since 2009 — 1,761 km from Kazansky station to the subtropical Black Sea platforms of Adler in 23 hours.

properties.name: Yantar
properties.highlight: Running since 1964, the Yantar threads Moscow to Russia’s Baltic exclave via Minsk and Vilnius — the last corridor where Russian passenger trains still cross the EU.

properties.name: Sakhalin Night Train
properties.highlight: Russia’s island sleeper: 613 km up Sakhalin’s east coast to oil-town Nogliki, on a railway begun under Japanese rule that kept its narrow gauge until regauging in 2019.

properties.name: AYaM Yakutia Train
properties.highlight: 23 hours north from the BAM at Tynda across permafrost to Nizhny Bestyakh on the Lena, one river crossing short of Yakutsk — Russia’s newest long-distance railway.

properties.name: Zhemchuzhina Kavkaza
properties.highlight: A hotel on rails looping from Moscow around the North Caucasus — Maykop, Kislovodsk, Vladikavkaz, Grozny, seaside Derbent and Nalchik — seven days, one ticket, since 2021.
